{"name": "performance_management_policy", "title": "PERFORMANCE MANAGEMENT POLICY", "content": "# WEAVER ENTERPRISES INC.\n## PERFORMANCE MANAGEMENT POLICY\n\n**Document Number:** HR-POL-007  \n**Effective Date:** January 1, 2024  \n**Last Revised:** January 1, 2024  \n**Policy Owner:** Human Resources Department  \n**Approved By:** Chief Human Resources Officer\n\n---\n\n## 1. PURPOSE AND PHILOSOPHY\n\nWeaver Enterprises is committed to fostering a culture of continuous improvement, development, and achievement. Our performance management process is designed to:\n\n- Align individual performance with organizational goals\n- Provide regular feedback and coaching\n- Recognize and reward strong performance\n- Identify and address performance gaps\n- Support employee development and career growth\n- Make informed decisions about compensation, promotions, and succession planning\n\nPerformance management is an ongoing dialogue between managers and employees, not a once-a-year event. This policy establishes the framework for that ongoing conversation.\n\n## 2. SCOPE AND APPLICABILITY\n\nThis policy applies to all regular full-time and part-time employees. Temporary employees and interns follow a modified process as determined by their manager and HR.\n\n**Probationary Period:**\n- New hires are in a 90-day probationary period\n- Performance evaluated at 30, 60, and 90 days\n- Different evaluation process than annual review\n- Successful completion required for continued employment\n\n## 3. PERFORMANCE MANAGEMENT CYCLE\n\n### 3.1 Annual Cycle Overview\n\nThe performance management cycle follows the calendar year:\n\n| Activity | Timing | Participants |\n|----------|--------|--------------|\n| Goal Setting | January - February | Employee & Manager |\n| Mid-Year Review | June - July | Employee & Manager |\n| Self-Assessment | November | Employee |\n| Annual Performance Review | December - January | Employee, Manager, & HR |\n| Calibration | January | Leadership Team |\n| Performance Ratings Finalized | January | Manager & HR |\n| Compensation Decisions | February | Manager, HR, & Finance |\n\n### 3.2 Continuous Feedback\n\nIn addition to formal reviews, managers should provide:\n- Real-time feedback on specific situations\n- Regular one-on-one meetings (at least bi-weekly)\n- Informal check-ins on progress toward goals\n- Recognition for achievements and milestones\n- Constructive guidance when performance concerns arise\n\n## 4. GOAL SETTING\n\n### 4.1 SMART Goals Framework\n\nAll performance goals should be SMART:\n- **S**pecific - Clear and unambiguous\n- **M**easurable - Can track progress and results\n- **A**chievable - Realistic given resources and constraints\n- **R**elevant - Aligned with team and company objectives\n- **T**ime-bound - Has defined timeline or deadline\n\n### 4.2 Types of Goals\n\n**Performance Goals:**\n- What the employee will accomplish\n- Specific deliverables and outcomes\n- Key projects and initiatives\n- Quality and productivity targets\n\n**Development Goals:**\n- Skills to build or enhance\n- Learning and training activities\n- Stretch assignments or new responsibilities\n- Career development objectives\n\n**Behavioral Goals:**\n- Demonstration of company values\n- Leadership competencies\n- Collaboration and teamwork\n- Communication and interpersonal skills\n\n### 4.3 Goal Setting Process\n\n**Employee Preparation:**\n- Review company and department objectives\n- Reflect on past performance and accomplishments\n- Identify priorities for upcoming year\n- Consider development areas and career aspirations\n- Draft proposed goals\n\n**Manager-Employee Discussion:**\n- Review organizational priorities\n- Discuss employee's proposed goals\n- Ensure alignment with team objectives\n- Confirm goals are SMART\n- Agree on success criteria\n- Document final goals in performance system\n\n**Typical Number of Goals:**\n- 3-5 performance goals\n- 1-2 development goals\n- All employees have behavioral expectations based on company values\n\n### 4.4 Cascading Goals\n\nGoals should cascade from organizational to individual level:\n1. Company sets strategic objectives\n2. Departments align goals to company strategy\n3. Teams align goals to department objectives\n4. Individuals align goals to team priorities\n\nThis ensures everyone's work contributes to organizational success.\n\n### 4.5 Adjusting Goals Mid-Year\n\nGoals may be adjusted when:\n- Business priorities change significantly\n- Role responsibilities change\n- External circumstances require pivot\n- Goals proven unrealistic or too easy\n\n**Process for Adjusting Goals:**\n- Manager and employee discuss need for change\n- Document rationale for adjustment\n- Update goals in performance system\n- HR approval required for significant changes\n\n## 5. MID-YEAR REVIEW\n\n### 5.1 Purpose\n\nThe mid-year review is a formal checkpoint to:\n- Assess progress toward annual goals\n- Provide feedback on performance to date\n- Identify any obstacles or support needed\n- Adjust goals if necessary\n- Discuss development and career aspirations\n\n### 5.2 Mid-Year Review Process\n\n**Employee Preparation:**\n- Review goals set at beginning of year\n- Assess progress on each goal\n- Identify accomplishments and challenges\n- Prepare questions or discussion topics\n\n**Manager Preparation:**\n- Review employee's goals and documentation\n- Assess progress and performance\n- Gather examples of performance (positive and developmental)\n- Prepare feedback and coaching points\n\n**Mid-Year Discussion:**\n- Review progress on each goal\n- Discuss what's working well\n- Address challenges or obstacles\n- Identify support or resources needed\n- Adjust goals if necessary\n- Discuss development progress\n\n**Documentation:**\n- Manager documents key discussion points\n- Updates goal progress in system\n- Notes any agreed-upon actions or changes\n\n### 5.3 Mid-Year Ratings\n\nMid-year reviews are developmental, not evaluative:\n- No formal performance rating assigned\n- Focus on coaching and improvement\n- Informal assessment of on-track vs. off-track\n- Manager should be transparent about concerns\n\n## 6. ANNUAL PERFORMANCE REVIEW\n\n### 6.1 Self-Assessment\n\n**Employee Responsibilities:**\n- Complete self-assessment in November\n- Evaluate performance against each goal\n- Provide evidence of accomplishments\n- Reflect on strengths and development areas\n- Identify learning and growth during the year\n- Suggest rating (optional)\n\n**Self-Assessment Questions:**\n- What were your key accomplishments this year?\n- How did you progress on each goal?\n- What challenges did you face and how did you address them?\n- How did you demonstrate company values?\n- What did you learn this year?\n- What are your development priorities for next year?\n\n### 6.2 Manager Assessment\n\n**Manager Responsibilities:**\n- Review employee self-assessment\n- Evaluate performance against goals and expectations\n- Gather input from peers and stakeholders (360 feedback when applicable)\n- Consider performance throughout entire year, not just recent months\n- Document specific examples supporting rating\n- Draft performance review comments\n\n**Manager Assessment Considers:**\n- Achievement of performance goals\n- Quality of work and deliverables\n- Demonstration of company values\n- Growth and development\n- Contribution to team success\n- Initiative and problem-solving\n- Collaboration and communication\n\n### 6.3 Performance Review Discussion\n\n**Scheduling:**\n- Schedule 60-90 minutes in private setting\n- Allow adequate time for discussion\n- Avoid end of day or rushed timing\n- Choose comfortable, confidential location\n\n**Discussion Structure:**\n\n1. **Opening (5 minutes)**\n   - Set positive, constructive tone\n   - Review purpose and format\n   - Put employee at ease\n\n2. **Employee Self-Reflection (15 minutes)**\n   - Employee shares self-assessment\n   - Discuss accomplishments and challenges\n   - Manager listens actively, asks questions\n\n3. **Manager Feedback (30 minutes)**\n   - Review each goal and overall performance\n   - Provide specific examples\n   - Discuss strengths and areas for improvement\n   - Share performance rating and rationale\n   - Allow employee to respond and discuss\n\n4. **Development Discussion (15 minutes)**\n   - Discuss career aspirations\n   - Identify development opportunities\n   - Plan for skill-building\n   - Discuss potential next roles or growth paths\n\n5. **Looking Ahead (15 minutes)**\n   - Preview next year's priorities\n   - Discuss expectations and goals\n   - Identify support needed\n   - End on positive, forward-looking note\n\n6. **Closing (5 minutes)**\n   - Summarize key points\n   - Confirm next steps\n   - Allow for final questions\n   - Thank employee for contributions\n\n**Documentation:**\n- Manager completes written review in system\n- Employee can add comments or feedback\n- Both manager and employee acknowledge review\n- HR reviews for completeness and consistency\n\n### 6.4 Calibration Process\n\n**Purpose of Calibration:**\n- Ensure consistency in ratings across organization\n- Reduce bias and promote fairness\n- Align on performance standards\n- Validate proposed ratings before finalization\n\n**Calibration Meetings:**\n- Held at department or division level\n- Led by department head with HR facilitation\n- Managers discuss team members' performance\n- Compare employees at similar levels\n- Adjust ratings if inconsistencies identified\n\n**Calibration Guidelines:**\n- Focus on performance evidence, not opinions\n- Consider full year, not recent events only\n- Apply rating criteria consistently\n- Challenge ratings that seem inflated or deflated\n- Document rationale for any rating changes\n\n## 7. PERFORMANCE RATINGS\n\n### 7.1 Rating Scale\n\nWeaver Enterprises uses a 5-point rating scale:\n\n**5 - Exceptional:**\n- Consistently exceeds expectations in all areas\n- Demonstrates extraordinary impact\n- Serves as role model for others\n- Achieves exceptional results against challenging goals\n- *Typical distribution: 5-10% of employees*\n\n**4 - Exceeds Expectations:**\n- Regularly exceeds expectations\n- Achieves all goals and often goes beyond\n- Strong demonstration of company values\n- Makes significant contributions to team success\n- *Typical distribution: 15-20% of employees*\n\n**3 - Meets Expectations:**\n- Consistently meets all performance expectations\n- Achieves goals and objectives\n- Demonstrates company values\n- Reliable, solid contributor\n- *Typical distribution: 60-70% of employees*\n\n**2 - Partially Meets Expectations:**\n- Performance meets some but not all expectations\n- Some goals achieved, others not met\n- Improvement needed in specific areas\n- May be new to role or developing skills\n- *Typical distribution: 5-10% of employees*\n\n**1 - Does Not Meet Expectations:**\n- Performance consistently below expectations\n- Most goals not achieved\n- Significant improvement required\n- May result in performance improvement plan or termination\n- *Typical distribution: Less than 5% of employees*\n\n### 7.2 Rating Guidelines\n\n**What Ratings Mean:**\n\nA **\"Meets Expectations\" (3)** rating is a positive rating indicating:\n- Employee is performing job successfully\n- Meeting all core responsibilities\n- Contributing to team effectively\n- This is the expected and most common rating\n\n**Exceeds Expectations (4) or Exceptional (5)** should be reserved for:\n- Truly outstanding performance\n- Going significantly beyond job requirements\n- Delivering exceptional results\n- Making substantial impact on organization\n\n**Below Expectations (2 or 1)** indicates:\n- Performance concerns requiring attention\n- Goals not being met\n- Need for improvement plan\n- Potential risk to continued employment (for rating of 1)\n\n### 7.3 Rating Distribution\n\nWhile not enforced as strict quotas, expected rating distributions help ensure:\n- Consistency across organization\n- Meaningful differentiation\n- Alignment with performance reality\n- Fair compensation decisions\n\nManagers should discuss with HR if their team's distribution significantly deviates from guidelines.\n\n## 8. FEEDBACK AND COACHING\n\n### 8.1 Effective Feedback\n\n**Characteristics of Good Feedback:**\n- Specific and concrete (not vague)\n- Timely (close to the event)\n- Focused on behavior, not personality\n- Balanced (strengths and development areas)\n- Actionable (includes suggestions for improvement)\n- Two-way (allows for discussion)\n\n**Positive Feedback:**\n- Recognize specific accomplishments\n- Explain impact of the behavior\n- Encourage continued performance\n- Provide publicly when appropriate\n\n**Constructive Feedback:**\n- Deliver privately\n- Focus on specific behavior or outcome\n- Explain impact or concern\n- Discuss alternative approaches\n- Create action plan for improvement\n- Follow up on progress\n\n### 8.2 SBI Feedback Model\n\nUse the Situation-Behavior-Impact model:\n\n**Situation:** Describe when and where\n- \"In yesterday's client meeting...\"\n\n**Behavior:** Describe what you observed\n- \"...you interrupted the client multiple times...\"\n\n**Impact:** Explain the effect\n- \"...which made them seem frustrated and cut the meeting short.\"\n\nThen discuss and problem-solve together.\n\n### 8.3 Regular One-on-Ones\n\nManagers should hold regular one-on-one meetings with direct reports:\n\n**Frequency:** At least bi-weekly (weekly preferred)\n\n**Duration:** 30-60 minutes\n\n**Employee-Driven Agenda:**\n- Progress updates on goals and projects\n- Obstacles or challenges\n- Questions or support needed\n- Ideas and suggestions\n- Career development\n\n**Manager's Role:**\n- Listen actively\n- Provide coaching and guidance\n- Remove obstacles\n- Offer feedback\n- Discuss priorities\n\n**Documentation:**\n- Keep brief notes on key discussion points\n- Track action items and commitments\n- Note performance observations over time\n\n## 9. PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLANS (PIP)\n\n### 9.1 When PIPs Are Used\n\nPerformance Improvement Plans are formal interventions used when:\n- Employee receives \"Does Not Meet Expectations\" rating\n- Performance issues are serious or ongoing\n- Previous coaching has not resulted in improvement\n- Clear performance standards are not being met\n\nPIPs are not appropriate for:\n- Minor performance issues (use coaching)\n- First-time mistakes or errors\n- Short-term performance dips\n- Misconduct (use disciplinary process instead)\n\n### 9.2 PIP Process\n\n**Step 1: Planning**\n- Manager consults with HR before initiating PIP\n- Review documentation of performance issues\n- Ensure concerns are job-related and documented\n- Determine if PIP is appropriate intervention\n\n**Step 2: PIP Development**\n- Identify specific performance deficiencies\n- Set clear, measurable improvement goals\n- Define timeline (typically 30-90 days)\n- Specify support and resources provided\n- Outline consequences if improvement not achieved\n\n**Step 3: PIP Meeting**\n- Manager meets with employee privately\n- Review performance concerns with specific examples\n- Present written PIP document\n- Explain expectations and timeline\n- Discuss support available\n- Allow employee to ask questions\n- Employee signs acknowledgment (signature = receipt, not agreement)\n\n**Step 4: Monitoring and Support**\n- Regular check-ins (weekly recommended)\n- Provide ongoing feedback\n- Document progress or lack thereof\n- Adjust support if needed\n- Keep HR informed\n\n**Step 5: PIP Conclusion**\n- Evaluate performance at end of PIP period\n- Meet with employee to discuss results\n- Possible outcomes:\n  - **Successful:** Employee meets goals, PIP closed, return to normal performance management\n  - **Partially Successful:** Extend PIP with revised goals\n  - **Unsuccessful:** Performance still deficient, proceed to termination\n\n### 9.3 PIP Documentation Requirements\n\n**PIP Document Must Include:**\n- Specific performance deficiencies with examples\n- Clear performance standards expected\n- Measurable goals for improvement\n- Timeline and check-in schedule\n- Support and resources provided\n- Consequences for non-improvement\n- Manager and employee signatures\n\n**Ongoing Documentation:**\n- Weekly check-in notes\n- Specific examples of improvement or continued deficiency\n- Any additional coaching or support provided\n- Final evaluation at end of PIP period\n\n### 9.4 Employee Rights During PIP\n\nEmployees on PIP:\n- Have right to understand specific concerns\n- Can ask questions and seek clarification\n- May provide their perspective\n- Should receive regular feedback during PIP\n- Can request HR involvement or guidance\n- May add comments to PIP documentation\n\n## 10. COMPENSATION AND PERFORMANCE\n\n### 10.1 Link to Compensation\n\nPerformance ratings influence compensation decisions:\n\n**Merit Increases:**\n- Annual merit increase budget allocated by performance rating\n- Higher performers receive larger percentage increases\n- Typical merit increase guidelines (when budget allows):\n  - Exceptional (5): 4-6% increase\n  - Exceeds Expectations (4): 3-4% increase\n  - Meets Expectations (3): 2-3% increase\n  - Partially Meets (2): 0-1% increase\n  - Does Not Meet (1): No increase\n\n**Bonuses:**\n- Performance ratings determine bonus eligibility and amount\n- Bonus targets vary by level and role\n- Individual performance rating multiplied by bonus target\n- Company performance also factors into bonus funding\n\n### 10.2 Promotions\n\nPromotions based on:\n- Consistent strong performance (typically multiple years of Exceeds or Exceptional ratings)\n- Demonstrated readiness for next level\n- Operating at next level for sustained period\n- Availability of role at next level\n- Business need and budget\n\nPromotion \u2260 automatic. Strong performance is necessary but not sufficient for promotion.\n\n### 10.3 Timing Considerations\n\n**Compensation Review Cycle:**\n- Performance reviews completed: December-January\n- Calibration and finalization: January\n- Compensation decisions: February\n- Merit increases effective: March 1\n- Bonuses paid: March (for prior year performance)\n\n**Other Timing Factors:**\n- Employees hired or promoted mid-year: prorated merit increase\n- Employees on leave during review period: reviewed upon return\n- Employees who change roles: reviewed by most recent manager with input from prior manager\n\n## 11. DEVELOPMENT AND CAREER GROWTH\n\n### 11.1 Individual Development Plans (IDP)\n\nAll employees encouraged to create Individual Development Plans:\n\n**IDP Components:**\n- Career goals and aspirations\n- Skills and competencies to develop\n- Development activities and resources\n- Timeline and milestones\n- Manager support needed\n\n**Development Activities:**\n- Formal training (courses, workshops, certifications)\n- On-the-job learning (stretch assignments, job rotation)\n- Mentoring and coaching\n- Professional associations and conferences\n- Reading and self-study\n- Peer learning and shadowing\n\n### 11.2 Career Conversations\n\nManagers should discuss career development at least annually:\n- Employee's career aspirations and interests\n- Potential career paths within organization\n- Skills and experiences needed for advancement\n- Development opportunities available\n- Realistic timeline for progression\n- Manager's commitment to support growth\n\n### 11.3 Succession Planning\n\nSuccession planning identifies and develops future leaders:\n- High-potential employees identified\n- Development plans created for critical roles\n- Cross-training and knowledge transfer\n- Leadership development programs\n- Mentoring by senior leaders\n\n## 12. ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES\n\n### 12.1 Employee Responsibilities\n\n- Set meaningful goals aligned with team objectives\n- Complete self-assessments honestly and thoroughly\n- Seek feedback and coaching regularly\n- Take ownership of performance and development\n- Communicate proactively about challenges\n- Participate constructively in performance discussions\n- Follow through on commitments and improvement plans\n\n### 12.2 Manager Responsibilities\n\n- Provide clear expectations and goals\n- Give regular, specific feedback\n- Document performance throughout year\n- Conduct fair, objective performance reviews\n- Develop and coach direct reports\n- Differentiate performance appropriately\n- Address performance issues promptly\n- Support employee career development\n- Complete all performance management activities on time\n\n### 12.3 HR Responsibilities\n\n- Design and maintain performance management process\n- Provide training and tools for managers and employees\n- Ensure compliance and consistency\n- Facilitate calibration sessions\n- Review performance documentation\n- Support managers with difficult performance situations\n- Analyze performance data and trends\n- Continuously improve the process\n\n### 12.4 Senior Leadership Responsibilities\n\n- Model effective performance management\n- Hold managers accountable for quality performance management\n- Participate in calibration for their organizations\n- Make final decisions on ratings and compensation\n- Support performance culture through actions and communications\n\n## 13. PERFORMANCE MANAGEMENT TRAINING\n\n### 13.1 Required Training\n\n**For All Employees:**\n- Performance management overview (1 hour)\n- Goal setting workshop (2 hours)\n- Included in new hire orientation\n\n**For All Managers:**\n- Manager essentials: Performance management (4 hours)\n- Conducting effective performance reviews (3 hours)\n- Coaching and feedback skills (4 hours)\n- Managing difficult performance conversations (2 hours)\n- Annual refresher (2 hours)\n\n**For New Managers:**\n- Complete all manager training within first 90 days\n- Shadow experienced manager during performance review\n- HR support for first performance cycle\n\n### 13.2 Available Resources\n\n**Performance Management System:**\n- Online portal for goals, reviews, and documentation\n- Templates and examples\n- Training videos and guides\n- Mobile app for on-the-go access\n\n**Manager Resources:**\n- Performance review writing guide\n- Sample language and phrases\n- Difficult conversation scripts\n- PIP templates\n- One-on-one meeting agendas\n\n**Employee Resources:**\n- Self-assessment templates\n- Career development planning tools\n- Learning and development catalog\n- Goal-setting worksheets\n\n## 14. QUESTIONS AND SUPPORT\n\nFor questions about performance management:\n\n**HR Business Partners:**\n- Consulting on performance issues\n- Coaching for difficult conversations\n- Review of performance documentation\n- PIP guidance and support\n\n**Learning & Development:**\n- Training on performance management\n- Coaching skills development\n- Career development resources\n\n**HR Service Center:**\n- Phone: (555) 123-4567\n- Email: hr@weaverenterprises.com\n- Performance management system support\n\n---\n\n*This policy is subject to change. Weaver Enterprises reserves the right to modify the performance management process as needed to meet business objectives.*"}, {"name": "recruiting_policy", "title": "Interview Process", "content": "# Amazon Recruiting Policy\n\nThis document outlines the recruiting and hiring policies for Amazon.\n\n## Interview Process\n\n### Interview Stages\n\n1. **Application Review** - Initial screening of applications\n2. **Phone Screen** - 30-minute phone interview with recruiter\n3. **Technical Interview** - 60-minute technical assessment\n4. **Behavioral Interview** - 45-minute behavioral assessment\n5. **Panel Interview** - 90-minute interview with multiple interviewers\n6. **Final Round** - 120-minute final assessment (may include multiple sessions)\n\n### Interview Scheduling\n\n- Interviews should be scheduled within 2 business days of previous stage completion\n- Interview rooms must be reserved at least 24 hours in advance\n- Video interviews are available for remote candidates\n- Panel interviews require Conference Room A or Panel Interview Room\n\n### Interview Rooms\n\n- **Conference Room A** - Main interview room, capacity 6\n- **Conference Room B** - Secondary interview room, capacity 4\n- **Video Conference Room** - Equipped for remote interviews\n- **Panel Interview Room** - Large room for panel interviews, capacity 10\n\n## Candidate Pipeline Management\n\n### Pipeline Stages\n\n- Application Review\n- Phone Screen Scheduled\n- Phone Screen Completed\n- Technical Interview Scheduled\n- Technical Interview Completed\n- Panel Interview Scheduled\n- Panel Interview Completed\n- Offer Extended\n- Offer Accepted\n- Offer Declined\n- Rejected\n\n### Candidate Communication\n\n- All candidate communications should be sent via email\n- Response time should be within 24 hours for candidate inquiries\n- Interview confirmations should be sent at least 48 hours in advance\n- Rejection notifications should be sent promptly after decision\n\n## Job Requisition Process\n\n1. Hiring manager creates job requisition\n2. Requisition reviewed and approved by VP of Talent Acquisition\n3. Job posting published\n4. Candidates apply\n5. Recruiting team screens and schedules interviews\n6. Hiring decision made\n7. Offer extended\n8. Onboarding process begins\n\n## Email Accounts\n\n### HR Team Email Addresses\n\n- **VP of Talent Acquisition**: sarah.johnson@amazon.com\n- **Talent Acquisition Manager**: michael.chen@amazon.com\n- **Senior Recruiter**: emily.davis@amazon.com\n- **Recruiter**: james.brown@amazon.com\n- **Recruiter**: alexandra.rodriguez@amazon.com\n- **Recruiting Operations Manager**: jennifer.martinez@amazon.com\n- **Recruiting Coordinator**: robert.wilson@amazon.com\n- **Scheduling Specialist**: patricia.lee@amazon.com\n- **Onboarding Manager**: david.thompson@amazon.com\n- **Onboarding Specialist**: lisa.anderson@amazon.com\n- **HRIS Coordinator**: kevin.park@amazon.com\n\n### General Email Addresses\n\n- **Recruiting Department**: recruiting@amazon.com\n- **HR Department**: hr@amazon.com\n"}, {"name": "recruitment_hiring_policy", "title": "RECRUITMENT & HIRING POLICY", "content": "# AMAZON INC.\n## RECRUITMENT & HIRING POLICY\n\n**Document Number:** HR-POL-006  \n**Effective Date:** January 1, 2024  \n**Last Revised:** January 1, 2024  \n**Policy Owner:** Human Resources Department  \n**Approved By:** Chief Human Resources Officer\n\n---\n\n## 1. PURPOSE AND SCOPE\n\nThis policy establishes guidelines and procedures for recruiting, selecting, and hiring qualified employees at Amazon Inc. The purpose is to:\n\n- Attract and hire highly qualified candidates\n- Ensure fair, consistent, and legally compliant hiring practices\n- Promote diversity and equal employment opportunity\n- Create positive candidate experiences\n- Support business objectives through strategic talent acquisition\n\nThis policy applies to all hiring activities for regular full-time, part-time, temporary, and intern positions across all departments and locations.\n\n## 2. EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY\n\n### 2.1 Non-Discrimination Commitment\n\nAmazon is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All employment decisions are made without regard to:\n- Race, color, ethnicity, national origin\n- Sex, gender, gender identity, gender expression\n- Sexual orientation\n- Religion, creed\n- Age\n- Disability (physical or mental)\n- Genetic information\n- Pregnancy or related conditions\n- Military or veteran status\n- Marital status\n- Any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law\n\n### 2.2 Affirmative Action\n\nThe Company is committed to affirmative action and takes proactive steps to ensure equal opportunity in recruitment, hiring, and advancement. Specific affirmative action plans are maintained as required by federal contractors regulations.\n\n### 2.3 Reasonable Accommodation\n\nThe Company provides reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities during the application and interview process. Candidates requiring accommodations should contact the HR Recruiting team.\n\n## 3. HIRING AUTHORITY AND APPROVAL\n\n### 3.1 Position Authorization\n\nBefore recruiting for any position:\n- Department head must obtain budget approval\n- Submit headcount request to HR with business justification\n- HR reviews and obtains necessary approvals (Finance, Executive Leadership)\n- Position approved for posting once all approvals obtained\n\n### 3.2 Job Requisition Process\n\n**Hiring Manager Responsibilities:**\n1. Complete job requisition form in applicant tracking system\n2. Provide or update job description\n3. Specify required qualifications and preferences\n4. Indicate salary range and position level\n5. Submit to HR for review\n\n**HR Review:**\n- Verify budget availability\n- Review job description for compliance and clarity\n- Confirm compensation is market-competitive\n- Approve requisition or return for revisions\n- Post approved positions internally and externally\n\n## 4. JOB DESCRIPTIONS\n\n### 4.1 Purpose of Job Descriptions\n\nJob descriptions serve as foundation for:\n- Recruiting and screening candidates\n- Setting performance expectations\n- Determining compensation levels\n- Conducting performance evaluations\n- Compliance with employment laws (ADA, FLSA)\n\n### 4.2 Required Components\n\nAll job descriptions must include:\n- Job title and department\n- FLSA classification (exempt or non-exempt)\n- Reporting relationship\n- Position summary\n- Essential job functions and duties\n- Required qualifications (education, experience, skills)\n- Preferred qualifications\n- Physical requirements and working conditions\n- Travel requirements (if applicable)\n\n### 4.3 Creating and Updating Job Descriptions\n\n- HR maintains template and assists with development\n- Hiring managers draft or update descriptions\n- HR reviews for compliance and consistency\n- Approved job descriptions stored in HR system\n- Review and update annually or when position changes significantly\n\n## 5. SOURCING AND RECRUITING\n\n### 5.1 Internal Posting\n\n**Internal Candidates First:**\n- All positions posted internally for minimum 5 business days before external posting\n- Posted on internal careers portal and distributed via employee email\n- Current employees encouraged to apply for advancement opportunities\n\n**Internal Applicant Process:**\n- Employees apply through internal portal\n- Notify current manager before applying (encouraged but not required)\n- If selected, coordinate transition timeline with both managers\n- HR facilitates internal transfers\n\n**Internal Transfer Guidelines:**\n- Employees must be in current role for 12 months before applying for new position\n- Exception: restructuring, elimination, or manager approval\n- Performance must be satisfactory (not on performance improvement plan)\n\n### 5.2 External Recruiting Sources\n\n**Company Career Website:**\n- Primary external recruiting source\n- Integrated with applicant tracking system\n- Mobile-optimized application process\n\n**Job Boards:**\n- General: Indeed, LinkedIn, Glassdoor\n- Industry-specific: As appropriate for role\n- Diversity-focused: Ability Jobs, Veterans Job Board, diversity professional associations\n\n**Employee Referrals:**\n- Employees encouraged to refer qualified candidates\n- Referral bonus program (see Section 5.3)\n- Referrals receive priority review\n\n**Recruiting Agencies:**\n- Used for specialized or hard-to-fill roles\n- Must be pre-approved vendor\n- Contingency or retained search depending on level\n- Typical fees: 20-25% of first-year compensation\n\n**Campus Recruiting:**\n- Target universities for entry-level and intern hiring\n- Career fairs, on-campus interviews, info sessions\n- Internship-to-full-time pipeline\n\n**Social Media:**\n- LinkedIn recruiting and company page\n- Twitter, Facebook for employer branding\n- Showcase company culture and opportunities\n\n**Professional Associations:**\n- Partner with industry organizations\n- Sponsor events and job boards\n- Attend conferences for networking\n\n### 5.3 Employee Referral Program\n\n**Referral Bonus Structure:**\n- Entry-level positions: $500\n- Mid-level positions: $1,000\n- Senior/specialized positions: $2,000\n- Executive positions: $3,000\n\n**Eligibility Requirements:**\n- Referring employee must be in good standing\n- Referred candidate hired as regular employee\n- Referred candidate completes 90-day probationary period\n- Bonus paid after successful completion of probation\n\n**Process:**\n- Submit referral through employee portal\n- Include candidate name, contact info, and position of interest\n- HR screens referred candidate\n- Referrer notified of hiring decision\n\n## 6. APPLICATION PROCESS\n\n### 6.1 Online Application System\n\nAll external candidates must apply through the company's applicant tracking system:\n- Submit resume/CV\n- Complete application form\n- Answer pre-screening questions\n- Provide voluntary EEO demographic information (kept confidential, not used in hiring decisions)\n- Submit cover letter (optional but encouraged)\n\n### 6.2 Application Materials\n\n**Required:**\n- Resume or CV\n- Completed application form\n\n**Optional:**\n- Cover letter\n- Portfolio or work samples (for applicable positions)\n- References (typically requested later in process)\n\n### 6.3 Application Deadline\n\n- Applications accepted until position filled\n- Posting may close early if sufficient qualified candidates\n- Candidates notified if position filled\n\n### 6.4 Application Confirmation\n\n- Automated confirmation email sent upon submission\n- Includes timeline expectations\n- Provides contact information for questions\n\n## 7. CANDIDATE SCREENING AND SELECTION\n\n### 7.1 Resume Review\n\n**HR Initial Screening:**\n- Review all applications for minimum qualifications\n- Disqualify clearly unqualified candidates\n- Forward qualified candidates to hiring manager\n- Timeline: Within 3-5 business days of application\n\n**Hiring Manager Review:**\n- Evaluate candidates against position requirements\n- Select candidates for phone screening\n- Provide feedback to HR on selected candidates\n\n### 7.2 Phone Screening\n\n**Purpose:**\n- Assess basic qualifications and fit\n- Clarify experience and career goals\n- Explain position and company\n- Gauge candidate interest and availability\n- Determine if in-person interview warranted\n\n**Conducted By:** HR Recruiter or Hiring Manager\n\n**Duration:** 20-30 minutes\n\n**Key Topics:**\n- Work history and relevant experience\n- Interest in position and company\n- Salary expectations\n- Availability and timeline\n- Work authorization status\n\n### 7.3 Interview Process\n\n**Interview Stages:**\n\n**First-Round Interview:**\n- Hiring manager and 1-2 team members\n- In-person, video, or phone (typically video for remote roles)\n- Duration: 45-60 minutes\n- Focus: Skills, experience, cultural fit\n\n**Second-Round Interview:**\n- Additional team members and stakeholders\n- May include senior leadership\n- Duration: 2-4 hours (multiple interviews)\n- May include skills assessment, presentation, or case study\n\n**Final Interview:**\n- Department head or executive leadership\n- Reserved for finalist candidates\n- Focus: Leadership potential, strategic thinking, culture alignment\n- Duration: 30-60 minutes\n\n**Panel Interviews:**\n- Used when appropriate\n- 2-4 interviewers\n- Coordinated questions to avoid redundancy\n- Consolidated feedback\n\n### 7.4 Interview Guidelines\n\n**Professional Conduct:**\n- Treat all candidates with respect and professionalism\n- Start and end on time\n- Provide clear information about next steps\n\n**Structured Interviews:**\n- Use standardized questions for all candidates\n- Focus on job-related competencies\n- Behavioral questions preferred (\"Tell me about a time when...\")\n- Avoid hypothetical questions when possible\n\n**Legal Compliance:**\n- Ask only job-related questions\n- Avoid questions about protected characteristics\n- Do not ask about arrest records (conviction records permissible)\n- Do not require disclosure of disabilities (may discuss accommodations needed to perform essential functions)\n\n**Interview Panel Diversity:**\n- Strive for diverse interview panels\n- Multiple perspectives improve hiring decisions\n- Reduces potential for bias\n\n## 8. CANDIDATE ASSESSMENTS\n\n### 8.1 Skills Testing\n\nPre-employment testing may be used when job-related:\n- Technical skills assessments (coding, software proficiency)\n- Writing samples\n- Presentations or case studies\n- Personality and work style assessments (advisory only, not determinative)\n\n**Testing Requirements:**\n- Must be validated and job-related\n- Applied consistently to all candidates for position\n- Accommodations provided for disabilities\n- Results kept confidential\n\n### 8.2 Work Samples and Assignments\n\n- May request work samples or complete assignments\n- Must be reasonable in scope (typically 1-3 hours maximum)\n- Provide clear instructions and expectations\n- Compensate if assignment is substantial or results used by Company\n\n### 8.3 Cognitive and Personality Assessments\n\n- Used as one data point in hiring decision\n- Cannot be sole basis for decision\n- Must be validated for position\n- Results shared with candidate upon request\n\n## 9. BACKGROUND CHECKS AND SCREENING\n\n### 9.1 Types of Background Checks\n\n**Standard Background Check (All Positions):**\n- Criminal history (7 years)\n- Employment verification\n- Education verification\n- Professional license verification (if applicable)\n\n**Extended Background Check (Leadership and Sensitive Positions):**\n- Credit history (for financial roles only)\n- Driving record (for driving positions)\n- Global sanctions and watch lists\n\n### 9.2 Background Check Process\n\n**Timing:**\n- Conducted after conditional offer extended\n- Must be completed before start date\n- Offer contingent on satisfactory results\n\n**Procedure:**\n1. Candidate receives conditional offer\n2. HR sends background check authorization and disclosure\n3. Candidate completes authorization form\n4. Third-party vendor conducts check\n5. Results reviewed by HR\n6. Adverse action process followed if necessary (see 9.3)\n\n**Candidate Rights:**\n- Receive pre-adverse action notice if concerns arise\n- Opportunity to dispute or explain findings\n- Receive copy of background report\n- Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA) rights provided\n\n### 9.3 Adverse Action Process\n\nIf background check reveals disqualifying information:\n\n1. **Pre-Adverse Action:**\n   - Provide candidate with copy of report\n   - Give notice of intent to take adverse action\n   - Allow 5 business days to respond\n   - Review any explanation or disputes\n\n2. **Final Adverse Action:**\n   - If decision to rescind offer, send adverse action notice\n   - Include copy of report and FCRA rights\n   - Document decision rationale\n\n### 9.4 Criminal History Considerations\n\n**Ban the Box Compliance:**\n- Do not ask about criminal history on application\n- Discuss only after conditional offer\n- Consider nature, recency, and relevance of conviction\n- Provide opportunity for individualized assessment\n\n**Disqualifying Convictions:**\n- Convictions directly related to job responsibilities\n- Recent violent crimes (within 7 years)\n- Determined on case-by-case basis\n- Legal consultation for complex situations\n\n### 9.5 Drug Testing\n\nDrug testing conducted post-offer for:\n- Safety-sensitive positions (as defined by DOT or company policy)\n- Positions requiring operation of machinery or vehicles\n- Positions with security clearance requirements\n\n**Testing Protocol:**\n- Urine drug screen (5- or 10-panel)\n- Conducted at certified lab\n- Medical Review Officer (MRO) reviews results\n- Positive results may disqualify candidate\n\n## 10. REFERENCE CHECKS\n\n### 10.1 When to Conduct Reference Checks\n\n- After final interview for top candidates\n- Before extending offer\n- Minimum 2-3 professional references\n\n### 10.2 Reference Check Process\n\n**Conducted By:** HR or Hiring Manager\n\n**Information to Gather:**\n- Relationship to candidate and dates\n- Job responsibilities and performance\n- Strengths and areas for development\n- Attendance and reliability\n- Eligibility for rehire\n- Any concerns or additional comments\n\n**Documentation:**\n- Complete reference check form for each reference\n- Document all responses\n- Maintain in recruitment file\n\n### 10.3 Providing References for Former Employees\n\nWhen contacted for reference on former Amazon employees:\n- Confirm dates of employment and title only\n- Direct all reference requests to HR\n- Do not provide performance information without signed authorization\n- HR may provide additional information with proper authorization\n\n## 11. OFFER PROCESS\n\n### 11.1 Salary Determination\n\n**Factors Considered:**\n- Market data and salary ranges for position\n- Candidate qualifications and experience\n- Internal equity (compared to current employees)\n- Budget constraints\n- Candidate's salary expectations\n\n**Approval Requirements:**\n- Offers within approved range: Hiring manager approval\n- Offers above range: HR and Finance approval required\n- Executive positions: CEO approval required\n\n### 11.2 Offer Letter Components\n\nWritten offer letters include:\n- Job title and department\n- Start date\n- Compensation (salary or hourly rate)\n- Bonus or commission structure (if applicable)\n- Benefits summary\n- Work location and schedule\n- Reporting relationship\n- Employment-at-will statement\n- Contingencies (background check, drug test, I-9 verification)\n- Offer expiration date (typically 3-5 business days)\n\n### 11.3 Extending the Offer\n\n**Process:**\n1. HR prepares offer letter\n2. Obtain necessary approvals\n3. Hiring manager makes verbal offer by phone\n4. HR sends written offer via email\n5. Candidate reviews and accepts or declines\n6. Candidate returns signed offer letter\n\n**Verbal Offer:**\n- Discuss all terms clearly\n- Answer questions\n- Confirm candidate acceptance before sending written offer\n- Reiterate offer is contingent on background check\n\n**Negotiation:**\n- Some negotiation expected for senior roles\n- Stay within approved salary range when possible\n- Consult HR before revising offer\n- Document final agreed-upon terms\n\n### 11.4 Offer Acceptance\n\n- Candidate signs and returns offer letter\n- HR processes new hire paperwork\n- Hiring manager notified of acceptance\n- Begin onboarding process\n\n### 11.5 Offer Decline\n\nIf candidate declines:\n- Request feedback on reason\n- Document in ATS\n- Return to candidate pool or restart search\n- Notify interview team\n\n## 12. PRE-EMPLOYMENT REQUIREMENTS\n\n### 12.1 Form I-9 Employment Eligibility Verification\n\n**Timing:** Within 3 business days of start date\n\n**Process:**\n- Employee completes Section 1 on or before first day\n- Employee presents acceptable documents (List A, or List B + List C)\n- HR completes Section 2 within 3 business days\n- Documents examined in person (or via authorized remote verification)\n\n**Acceptable Documents:**\n- List A: Passport, Permanent Resident Card, Employment Authorization Document\n- List B: Driver's license, State ID, School ID with photo\n- List C: Social Security card, Birth certificate\n\n**E-Verify:**\n- Company participates in E-Verify program\n- Cases created within 3 business days of hire\n- Resolves tentative non-confirmations with employee assistance\n\n### 12.2 Tax Forms\n\n**Required Forms:**\n- Form W-4 (Federal withholding)\n- State withholding forms (as applicable)\n- Direct deposit authorization\n\n**Timing:** Completed on or before first day of employment\n\n### 12.3 Pre-Employment Paperwork\n\n**New Hire Packet Includes:**\n- Emergency contact information\n- Benefits enrollment forms\n- Employee handbook acknowledgment\n- Code of conduct acknowledgment\n- Confidentiality/IP agreements (for applicable positions)\n- Arbitration agreement (where permitted by law)\n- Company policies acknowledgments\n\n## 13. CANDIDATE COMMUNICATION\n\n### 13.1 Timely Communication\n\n- Acknowledge applications within 24 hours (automated)\n- Update candidates within 2 weeks of each interview stage\n- Provide clear timeline expectations\n- Be responsive to candidate questions\n\n### 13.2 Rejection Communication\n\n**When to Notify:**\n- After resume screening\n- After phone screen\n- After in-person interview\n\n**How to Notify:**\n- Email for early-stage rejections\n- Phone call for final-round candidates (encouraged)\n- Use professional, respectful language\n- Offer general feedback when appropriate (avoid detailed critique)\n\n**Feedback Guidelines:**\n- Keep feedback high-level and constructive\n- Focus on qualifications and fit, not personal characteristics\n- Avoid detailed explanations that could be legally problematic\n- Encourage candidate to apply for future opportunities\n\n### 13.3 Candidate Experience\n\n**Best Practices:**\n- Respond promptly to inquiries\n- Provide clear job descriptions and expectations\n- Maintain professional, respectful communication\n- Coordinate interview schedules efficiently\n- Reimburse travel expenses for final-round candidates (when applicable)\n- Send thank-you notes after interviews\n- Provide feedback when requested (within appropriate limits)\n\n## 14. RECORDKEEPING AND COMPLIANCE\n\n### 14.1 Applicant Tracking\n\n- All recruitment activity documented in the applicant tracking system (ATS)\n- Includes: applications, resumes, interview notes, assessments, correspondence\n- Ensures compliance with recordkeeping requirements\n- Supports data-driven recruiting decisions\n\n### 14.2 Required Records\n\n**Maintain for All Applicants:**\n- Application materials\n- Resumes/CVs\n- Interview evaluations\n- Assessment results\n- Reference check notes\n- Hiring decision rationale\n\n**Retention Period:**\n- Hired employees: Duration of employment + 7 years\n- Not hired: 3 years from application date (or 2 years for federal contractors)\n- Adverse impact analyses: 2 years\n\n### 14.3 EEO and Affirmative Action Reporting\n\n**EEO-1 Report:**\n- Annual report to EEOC\n- Employee demographics by job category\n- Due: September 30 each year\n\n**AAP Analysis:**\n- Annual utilization analysis\n- Applicant flow data\n- Adverse impact analysis\n- Goal setting and action plans\n\n**OFCCP Compliance:**\n- Internet applicant records\n- Hiring statistics by protected class\n- Self-identification data (voluntary)\n\n### 14.4 Audits and Investigations\n\n**Internal Audits:**\n- HR conducts periodic recruiting compliance audits\n- Review job descriptions, postings, interview questions\n- Analyze hiring data for potential adverse impact\n- Corrective action taken as needed\n\n**External Audits:**\n- Cooperate with OFCCP, EEOC, or other agency reviews\n- Provide requested documentation\n- Legal counsel involved in responses\n\n## 15. SPECIAL HIRING SITUATIONS\n\n### 15.1 Rehiring Former Employees\n\n**Eligibility:**\n- Former employees eligible for rehire if left in good standing\n- Not eligible if terminated for cause\n- HR reviews personnel file before rehiring\n\n**Process:**\n- Follow standard application and interview process\n- May expedite screening if recently separated (within 6 months)\n- New I-9 required if more than 3 years since termination\n- No break in service credit for benefits (treated as new hire)\n\n### 15.2 Nepotism and Personal Relationships\n\n**Policy:**\n- Company permits hiring of relatives but with restrictions\n- Relatives cannot be in direct reporting relationship\n- Cannot work in same department if one supervises the other\n\n**Disclosure Required:**\n- Candidates must disclose existing relationships with employees\n- HR evaluates potential conflicts\n\n**Definitions:**\n- Relatives: spouse, domestic partner, parent, child, sibling, in-laws, step-relatives\n\n### 15.3 Contingent Workers\n\n**Temporary Employees:**\n- Hired through approved staffing agencies\n- Contract duration specified upfront\n- May be converted to regular employee after 6 months\n\n**Independent Contractors:**\n- Must meet IRS independent contractor criteria\n- Separate contracting process (not covered by this policy)\n- Legal review required\n\n### 15.4 Interns\n\n**Internship Program:**\n- Paid internships (comply with FLSA)\n- College students or recent graduates\n- 10-12 week summer program or part-time during school year\n- Conversion to full-time considered based on performance\n\n**Process:**\n- Campus recruiting and online applications\n- Structured interview process\n- Educational experience with meaningful projects\n- Mentor assigned\n\n## 16. HIRING MANAGER RESPONSIBILITIES\n\nHiring managers are responsible for:\n- Initiating requisitions and obtaining approvals\n- Reviewing resumes and selecting candidates\n- Conducting interviews in professional, compliant manner\n- Providing timely feedback to HR\n- Participating in hiring decisions\n- Coordinating with HR on offers and negotiations\n- Preparing for new hire onboarding\n- Conducting new hire orientation for team\n\n## 17. QUESTIONS AND RESOURCES\n\nFor questions about recruiting and hiring, contact:\n\n**Talent Acquisition Team:**\n- Phone: (555) 123-4590\n- Email: recruiting@weaverenterprises.com\n\n**HR Business Partners:**\n- Support hiring managers with recruitment strategy\n- Provide guidance on interviews and selection\n\n**Recruiting Resources:**\n- Applicant tracking system\n- Interview question bank\n- Job description templates\n- Recruiting metrics dashboard\n\n---\n\n*This policy is subject to change. Amazon reserves the right to modify procedures as needed to comply with changing laws and business needs.*"}, {"name": "skills", "title": "HRIS Updates", "content": "It is currently 2026-02-01T17:00:00Z (UTC). \n\n# HR Recruiting \u2014 Implicit Task Conventions\n\nThese are standard conventions that apply to every recruiting task.\n\n## HRIS Updates\n- Every action that changes candidate status, interview details, or offer terms must be logged in HRIS\n- Update the relevant HRIS fields needed for an accurate record\n\n## Calendar Events\n- Include timezone in all calendar events\n- Include all relevant participants on the invite\n\n## Stakeholder Communication\n- When a change affects someone, notify them (e.g., swapping an interviewer \u2192 notify the old and new interviewer)\n- When you complete an action someone requested, confirm it with them\n- Internal jargon, acronyms, and process terminology should not appear in candidate emails\n- Always check your email inbox at the start of a task \u2014 there may be context (candidate details, prior approvals, policy clarifications, or instructions from stakeholders) that affects how the task should be completed.\n\n## Privacy\n- Do not include compensation details in calendar invites or group communications\n- Medical, accommodation details, and other personal info should only be shared with need-to-know parties\n\n## Communication Channels & Authority\n\n### Chat \u2014 DMs and Seeded Channels\n- NPCs can receive and respond in 1-1 direct messages and in seeded Rocket.Chat channels.\n- If a task requires `#channel` usage, that channel must be present in `seed_group_channels`.\n- Agents cannot create channels/groups during execution.\n\n### Authority Deferral\n- When opinions or decisions conflict across people in a recruiting decision (e.g., hiring manager vs. recruiter vs. HR), defer to the higher authority for the given task according to the org chart. Do not average opinions or side with whoever responded first.\n"}]}, "total": 62}

"# WEAVER ENTERPRISES INC.\n## 401(k) RETIREMENT PLAN POLICY\n\n**Document Number:** HR-POL-005  \n**Effective Date:** January 1, 2024  \n**Last Revised:** January 1, 2024  \n**Policy Owner:** Human Resources Department  \n**Plan Administrator:** Fidelity Investments  \n**Approved By:** Chief Human Resources Officer\n\n---\n\n## 1. PLAN OVERVIEW\n\nThe Amazon Inc. 401(k) Retirement Savings Plan (\"the Plan\") is designed to help employees save for retirement through tax-advantaged contributions and Company matching contributions. The Plan is a defined contribution plan qualified under Section 401(k) of the Internal Revenue Code.\n\n### 1.1 Plan Benefits\n\n- Pre-tax and Roth after-tax contribution options\n- Employer matching contributions\n- Immediate vesting of Company match\n- Wide variety of investment options\n- Tax-deferred growth (or tax-free for Roth)\n- Portable - take your account with you if you leave\n- Loan and hardship withdrawal provisions\n- Professional investment advice available\n\n### 1.2 Plan Administrator\n\n**Fidelity Investments** serves as the Plan's recordkeeper and trustee.\n\n**Contact Information:**\n- Website: www.401k.com\n- Phone: 1-800-555-0401\n- Mobile App: Fidelity NetBenefits\n\n## 2. ELIGIBILITY AND ENROLLMENT\n\n### 2.1 Eligibility Requirements\n\n**Eligible Employees:**\n- All employees age 21 and older\n- Must complete 90 days of service\n- Eligibility begins on the first day of the month following completion of 90-day waiting period\n\n**Excluded Employees:**\n- Independent contractors\n- Leased employees\n- Non-resident aliens with no U.S.-source income\n- Union employees (unless union agreement provides for participation)\n\n### 2.2 Automatic Enrollment\n\n**Automatic Enrollment Feature:**\n- All eligible employees are automatically enrolled at 3% of eligible compensation\n- Enrollment occurs on eligibility date unless employee actively opts out\n- Contributions directed to Plan's default investment option (target-date fund based on age)\n- Employees may change contribution rate or opt out at any time\n\n**Automatic Increase Program (Optional):**\n- Employees may elect to automatically increase contribution by 1% each year\n- Increases occur each January 1st\n- Continues until reaching 10% or employee-specified maximum\n- Employees may opt in or out at any time\n\n### 2.3 How to Enroll or Make Changes\n\n**Online Enrollment:**\n1. Visit www.401k.com\n2. Log in using Social Security Number and PIN (provided after eligibility)\n3. Select contribution rate (1%-100% of eligible pay)\n4. Choose contribution type (pre-tax, Roth, or both)\n5. Select investments or use professionally managed option\n6. Designate beneficiaries\n\n**Phone Enrollment:**\n- Call Fidelity at 1-800-555-0401\n- Representatives available Monday-Friday, 8am-10pm ET\n\n**Changes Effective:**\n- Enrollment or changes submitted by 4pm ET take effect within 1-2 pay periods\n\n## 3. CONTRIBUTIONS\n\n### 3.1 Employee Contributions\n\n**Contribution Types:**\n\n**Pre-Tax Contributions:**\n- Deducted from pay before income taxes\n- Reduces current taxable income\n- Taxes paid upon withdrawal in retirement\n- Growth is tax-deferred\n\n**Roth After-Tax Contributions:**\n- Deducted from pay after income taxes\n- Does not reduce current taxable income\n- Qualified withdrawals are tax-free in retirement (including earnings)\n- Must meet 5-year rule and be age 59\u00bd for tax-free withdrawal\n\nEmployees may contribute pre-tax, Roth, or a combination of both.\n\n### 3.2 Contribution Limits\n\n**2024 IRS Limits:**\n- Elective deferral limit: $23,000 per year\n- Catch-up contributions (age 50+): Additional $7,500 per year\n- Total catch-up limit for age 50+: $30,500 per year\n\n**Total Contribution Limit (employee + employer):**\n- $69,000 per year (or $76,500 with catch-up for age 50+)\n\n**Highly Compensated Employee Limits:**\n- Employees earning $155,000+ may be subject to additional IRS-imposed limits\n- Notifications sent if limits affect your contributions\n\n### 3.3 Eligible Compensation\n\nContributions are calculated as a percentage of eligible compensation:\n\n**Includes:**\n- Base salary or hourly wages\n- Overtime pay\n- Bonuses and commissions\n- Shift differentials\n\n**Excludes:**\n- Reimbursements and expense allowances\n- Employer contributions to benefit plans\n- Severance pay\n- Equity compensation (stock options, RSUs)\n\n**Annual Compensation Limit:**\n- IRS limits eligible compensation to $345,000 for 2024\n\n### 3.4 Changing Your Contributions\n\n- Change contribution percentage anytime online or by phone\n- Increase, decrease, or stop contributions at any time\n- Start contributions again at any time if previously opted out\n- Changes take effect within 1-2 pay periods\n\n## 4. EMPLOYER MATCHING CONTRIBUTIONS\n\n### 4.1 Company Match Formula\n\nAmazon provides a generous matching contribution:\n\n**Match Structure:**\n- 100% match on the first 3% of eligible compensation contributed\n- 50% match on the next 2% of eligible compensation contributed\n\n**Examples:**\n\n| Employee Contributes | Company Match | Total Contribution |\n|---------------------|---------------|-------------------|\n| 1% | 1% | 2% |\n| 3% | 3% | 6% |\n| 5% | 4% (3% + 0.5\u00d72%) | 9% |\n| 6%+ | 4% (maximum match) | 10%+ |\n\nTo receive the maximum Company match of 4%, employees must contribute at least 5% of eligible compensation.\n\n### 4.2 Match Timing\n\n- Company match contributions deposited each pay period\n- Match based on per-pay-period contributions (not annual total)\n- To maximize match, contribute consistently throughout the year\n\n### 4.3 Match Eligibility\n\n- All participating employees receive match (no waiting period)\n- Must be employed on the date contributions are made to receive match for that pay period\n- No match on contributions made after separation from Company\n\n### 4.4 True-Up Contribution\n\nThe Plan does not include an annual true-up contribution. To maximize the Company match, avoid front-loading contributions early in the year if you will reach the annual limit before year-end.\n\n## 5. VESTING\n\n### 5.1 Vesting Schedule\n\n**Employee Contributions:**\n- 100% vested immediately (pre-tax and Roth contributions)\n- Always fully own your own contributions and earnings\n\n**Employer Matching Contributions:**\n- 100% vested immediately\n- Full ownership from date of match deposit\n- No forfeiture upon termination\n\n**Immediate Vesting Benefit:**\n- All contributions (employee and employer) are fully vested at all times\n- No waiting period or service requirement\n- Take full account balance with you if you leave the Company\n\n## 6. INVESTMENT OPTIONS\n\n### 6.1 Available Investments\n\nThe Plan offers a diverse menu of investment options:\n\n**Target-Date Funds (Default Option):**\n- All-in-one diversified portfolios\n- Automatically adjust asset allocation as you near retirement\n- Available in 5-year increments (e.g., 2030, 2035, 2040)\n- Managed by Fidelity\n\n**Equity Funds:**\n- U.S. Large-Cap (growth and value)\n- U.S. Mid-Cap\n- U.S. Small-Cap\n- International Developed Markets\n- Emerging Markets\n\n**Fixed-Income Funds:**\n- Core Bond Fund\n- Short-Term Bond Fund\n- Inflation-Protected Securities\n- High-Yield Bond Fund\n\n**Specialty Funds:**\n- Real Estate (REIT)\n- Commodities\n- Stable Value Fund (capital preservation)\n\n**Self-Directed Brokerage Option:**\n- Access to thousands of additional investment options\n- Additional fees may apply\n- Requires separate election\n\n**Total Investment Options:** 20+ funds covering all major asset classes\n\n### 6.2 Fund Information\n\nDetailed information about each investment option, including:\n- Expense ratios\n- Historical performance\n- Risk ratings\n- Portfolio holdings\n- Fund fact sheets\n\nAvailable at www.401k.com or by calling 1-800-555-0401.\n\n### 6.3 Changing Investments\n\n**Future Contributions:**\n- Change investment elections for future contributions anytime\n- Changes effective next business day\n\n**Existing Balance (Fund Transfers):**\n- Transfer existing account balance between investments anytime\n- Unlimited transfers (no fees)\n- Transfers processed same business day (if submitted by 4pm ET)\n\n**Rebalancing:**\n- Automatic rebalancing available (quarterly, semi-annual, or annual)\n- Maintains target asset allocation automatically\n\n## 7. PROFESSIONAL INVESTMENT HELP\n\n### 7.1 Managed Accounts\n\n**Fidelity Personalized Planning & Advice:**\n- Professional portfolio management based on your goals, risk tolerance, and timeline\n- Ongoing monitoring and rebalancing\n- Annual fee: 0.40% of managed assets (charged quarterly)\n- Enroll online or call Fidelity\n\n### 7.2 Investment Guidance\n\n**Free Resources:**\n- Online retirement planning tools\n- Investment education library\n- Retirement income calculator\n- Asset allocation questionnaires\n\n**Fidelity Representatives:**\n- Phone guidance: 1-800-555-0401\n- One-on-one consultations (schedule through website)\n- Educational webinars throughout the year\n\n### 7.3 Financial Planning Tools\n\nAccess free tools at www.401k.com:\n- Retirement savings calculator\n- Social Security estimator\n- Budget and expense tracker\n- College savings calculator\n- Retirement income planner\n\n## 8. ACCESSING YOUR MONEY\n\n### 8.1 While Employed\n\n**In-Service Withdrawals:**\n\nGenerally, you cannot withdraw funds while actively employed, except:\n\n**Age 59\u00bd Withdrawals:**\n- Available once you reach age 59\u00bd\n- No penalty or special requirements\n- Taxes apply (unless Roth qualified withdrawals)\n\n**Hardship Withdrawals:**\n- Available for immediate and heavy financial need\n- Limited to employee contributions only (not earnings or match)\n- Must demonstrate financial hardship\n- Subject to taxes and 10% early withdrawal penalty (if under age 59\u00bd)\n- See Section 8.3 for qualifying hardships\n\n**Loans:**\n- Borrow from your own account\n- Must be repaid with interest\n- See Section 8.4 for loan provisions\n\n### 8.2 After Separation from Service\n\n**Distribution Options After Leaving Company:**\n\n**Option 1: Leave Money in Plan**\n- Keep account in Plan if balance exceeds $7,000\n- Continue to manage investments\n- No additional contributions allowed\n- Take distributions later when needed\n\n**Option 2: Rollover to IRA or New Employer's Plan**\n- Roll over to traditional IRA (pre-tax funds)\n- Roll over to Roth IRA (Roth funds, or convert pre-tax funds with taxes)\n- Roll over to new employer's 401(k) plan (if accepted)\n- Maintains tax-deferred/tax-free status\n- No taxes or penalties\n\n**Option 3: Cash Distribution**\n- Receive lump-sum payment\n- Subject to income taxes\n- 10% early withdrawal penalty if under age 59\u00bd (with exceptions)\n- 20% mandatory federal withholding\n\n**Automatic Cashout:**\n- If balance is $1,000-$7,000 and you don't make an election, automatically rolled to IRA\n- If balance is under $1,000, automatically cashed out and check mailed\n\n### 8.3 Hardship Withdrawals\n\n**Qualifying Hardship Reasons:**\n- Medical expenses for employee, spouse, or dependents\n- Costs related to purchase of primary residence (excluding mortgage payments)\n- Tuition and educational fees for next 12 months\n- Payments to prevent eviction or foreclosure on primary residence\n- Burial or funeral expenses for family members\n- Expenses to repair damage to primary residence (casualty loss)\n- Expenses and losses due to federally declared disaster\n\n**Requirements:**\n- Must demonstrate immediate and heavy financial need\n- Must have exhausted other resources (loans, insurance, other assets)\n- Amount limited to need (plus taxes and penalties)\n- Cannot contribute to Plan for 6 months after hardship withdrawal\n\n**Application Process:**\n- Complete hardship withdrawal request form\n- Provide supporting documentation\n- HR and plan administrator review and approve\n- Processing time: 7-10 business days\n\n### 8.4 Loans\n\n**Loan Provisions:**\n- Borrow minimum of $1,000\n- Borrow maximum of lesser of:\n  - $50,000 (reduced by highest outstanding loan balance in past 12 months), OR\n  - 50% of vested account balance\n- Maximum of 2 loans outstanding at any time\n\n**Loan Terms:**\n- General purpose loan: Up to 5-year repayment\n- Primary residence loan: Up to 15-year repayment\n- Interest rate: Prime rate + 1% (fixed at loan origination)\n- Interest paid back to your own account\n\n**Repayment:**\n- Automatic payroll deduction\n- Payments made at least quarterly\n- Level amortization (equal payments)\n\n**Fees:**\n- Loan origination fee: $75 (one-time)\n- Annual maintenance fee: $50 per loan\n- Fees deducted from loan proceeds or account balance\n\n**Defaulted Loans:**\n- Loan becomes taxable distribution if not repaid\n- 10% early withdrawal penalty applies if under age 59\u00bd\n- Loan defaults if:\n  - Payments not made for 90+ days\n  - Employment terminates (loan due within 60 days)\n\n**Application:**\n- Apply online at www.401k.com or call 1-800-555-0401\n- Funds typically available within 5 business days\n- Spouse consent required for loans over $5,000\n\n### 8.5 Required Minimum Distributions (RMDs)\n\n**When RMDs Begin:**\n- Age 73 (or 75 if born in 1960 or later)\n- Must begin by April 1 following year you turn required age\n- Exception: Can delay if still employed (and not 5% owner)\n\n**How RMDs Are Calculated:**\n- Based on IRS life expectancy tables\n- Calculated each year based on prior year-end balance\n- Fidelity calculates and notifies you of RMD amount\n\n**Tax Treatment:**\n- Pre-tax account RMDs are taxable income\n- Roth account RMDs are tax-free (if qualified)\n- Failure to take RMD results in 25% penalty on amount not taken\n\n## 9. BENEFICIARY DESIGNATIONS\n\n### 9.1 Importance of Beneficiary Designation\n\n- Designate who receives your account balance if you die\n- Beneficiaries avoid probate process\n- Can designate primary and contingent beneficiaries\n- Can split percentage among multiple beneficiaries\n\n### 9.2 Spousal Rights\n\n**If You Are Married:**\n- Spouse is automatic beneficiary unless spouse signs written consent to name someone else\n- Spousal consent must be notarized\n- Applies even if you designated someone else before marriage\n\n**If You Are Not Married:**\n- Can name anyone as beneficiary (person, trust, charity, estate)\n- No consent required\n\n### 9.3 Updating Beneficiaries\n\n- Review and update beneficiary designations regularly\n- Update after major life events: marriage, divorce, births, deaths\n- Update online at www.401k.com or by submitting paper form\n- Previous designation revoked by new designation\n\n### 9.4 If No Beneficiary Designated\n\nIf no beneficiary is designated or all beneficiaries predecease you:\n1. Spouse (if married)\n2. Children (equally)\n3. Parents (equally)\n4. Estate\n\n## 10. PLAN ADMINISTRATION\n\n### 10.1 Plan Year\n\nThe Plan operates on a calendar year (January 1 - December 31).\n\n### 10.2 Plan Administrator Responsibilities\n\n**Amazon Inc. serves as Plan Administrator:**\n- Interpret and administer Plan provisions\n- Make eligibility determinations\n- Process distributions and loans\n- Ensure compliance with federal regulations\n- Coordinate with Fidelity on recordkeeping\n\n### 10.3 Fiduciary Responsibilities\n\n**Plan Fiduciaries:**\n- Plan Administrator (Amazon)\n- Investment Committee (selects and monitors investment options)\n- Trustee (Fidelity Investments)\n\nFiduciaries act in best interest of Plan participants and beneficiaries.\n\n### 10.4 Plan Costs and Fees\n\n**Plan-Level Administrative Fees:**\n- Recordkeeping and administrative costs paid by Company\n- No direct charge to participant accounts for plan administration\n\n**Investment Fees:**\n- Each fund has an expense ratio (annual management fee)\n- Expense ratios range from 0.05% to 1.20% depending on fund\n- Deducted from fund returns (not separately billed)\n\n**Individual Transaction Fees:**\n- Loan origination: $75\n- Loan maintenance: $50 per year per loan\n- QDRO processing: $500\n- Overnight check delivery: $25\n- Paper statement fee: $2 per quarter (waived if opt for electronic)\n\n### 10.5 Plan Amendments and Termination\n\nAmazon reserves the right to:\n- Amend the Plan at any time\n- Terminate the Plan at any time\n- If Plan terminates, all accounts become 100% vested\n- Participants will be notified of significant changes\n\n## 11. SPECIAL SITUATIONS\n\n### 11.1 Military Leave\n\n**During Military Service:**\n- Can continue contributions if receiving pay\n- Employer match continues on contributions made\n- Can contribute missed deferrals when returning (within specified timeframe)\n\n**Returning from Military Service:**\n- Treated as continuously employed for vesting purposes\n- Eligible for makeup contributions based on military pay\n\n### 11.2 Leave of Absence\n\n**During Approved Unpaid Leave:**\n- Cannot make contributions (no compensation)\n- No employer match while not receiving pay\n- Account remains invested\n- Can make investment changes\n- Vesting service may continue depending on leave type (FMLA, military)\n\n### 11.3 Qualified Domestic Relations Orders (QDRO)\n\nIf you divorce, a court may order that part of your account be paid to your former spouse:\n- Order must be a Qualified Domestic Relations Order (QDRO)\n- Company reviews order to ensure it meets Plan and legal requirements\n- Once approved, account divided according to order\n- Former spouse (alternate payee) can roll over or take distribution\n\n**QDRO Review Fee:** $500 (charged to participant account)\n\n### 11.4 Disability\n\nIf you become disabled and receive long-term disability benefits:\n- Can continue contributions if receiving disability pay from Company\n- If disability pay ceases, contributions stop\n- Account remains invested\n- May access funds if disabled (before age 59\u00bd) without 10% penalty\n\n### 11.5 Death\n\n**Upon Participant Death:**\n- Beneficiaries notified and sent claim forms\n- Account balance paid to designated beneficiaries\n- Spousal beneficiaries have multiple options (keep in plan, rollover, distribute)\n- Non-spousal beneficiaries must take distributions (but can stretch over lifetime)\n\n## 12. IMPORTANT LEGAL INFORMATION\n\n### 12.1 Plan Documents\n\nThis policy summary provides an overview of the Plan. The official Plan Document and Summary Plan Description (SPD) govern in case of any discrepancy.\n\nDocuments available:\n- Summary Plan Description (SPD)\n- Plan Document\n- Trust Agreement\n- Annual notices\n\nAccess documents at: www.401k.com or request printed copy from HR.\n\n### 12.2 ERISA Rights\n\nAs a participant in the Plan, you are entitled to certain rights and protections under the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA):\n\n**Receive Information About Your Plan:**\n- Examine Plan documents at HR office\n- Obtain copies of Plan documents (reasonable fee may apply)\n- Receive Summary Annual Report (SAR) each year\n\n**Prudent Actions by Plan Fiduciaries:**\n- Plan fiduciaries must act prudently and in interest of participants\n- If fiduciaries misuse Plan assets, they can be held responsible\n\n**Enforce Your Rights:**\n- If claim is denied, right to appeal\n- Right to sue for benefits\n- Right to protection from retaliation for exercising ERISA rights\n\n### 12.3 Claims and Appeals\n\n**If Distribution Claim Is Denied:**\n- Receive written explanation within 90 days\n- Appeal denial within 60 days\n- Decision on appeal within 60 days\n- If appeal denied, may file suit in federal court\n\n## 13. TAX INFORMATION\n\n### 13.1 Tax Treatment of Contributions\n\n**Pre-Tax Contributions:**\n- Reduce taxable income in year contributed\n- Reported in Box 12 of W-2 with code D\n- Reduce federal income tax (and usually state)\n- Do not reduce Social Security or Medicare tax\n\n**Roth Contributions:**\n- Made with after-tax dollars\n- Do not reduce taxable income\n- Reported in Box 12 of W-2 with code AA\n\n### 13.2 Tax Treatment of Distributions\n\n**Pre-Tax Account Distributions:**\n- Fully taxable as ordinary income\n- Added to taxable income in year received\n- 10% early withdrawal penalty if under age 59\u00bd (with exceptions)\n\n**Roth Account Distributions:**\n- Contributions always tax-free\n- Earnings tax-free if qualified distribution (age 59\u00bd + 5-year rule)\n- Earnings taxable if non-qualified distribution\n- 10% penalty applies to taxable portion if under age 59\u00bd\n\n**Withholding:**\n- 20% mandatory federal withholding on eligible rollover distributions paid to you\n- Can avoid withholding by choosing direct rollover\n- State withholding may also apply\n\n### 13.3 Form 1099-R\n\n- Fidelity issues Form 1099-R by January 31 for prior year distributions\n- Reports taxable distributions to IRS\n- Use to prepare tax return\n- Keep for your records\n\n### 13.4 Consult a Tax Advisor\n\nThis summary provides general tax information. Individual tax situations vary. Consult a qualified tax advisor for advice specific to your situation.\n\n## 14. CONTACT INFORMATION\n\n### 14.1 Fidelity Investments (Plan Administrator)\n\n**Website:** www.401k.com\n**Phone:** 1-800-555-0401\n**Hours:** Monday-Friday, 8am-10pm ET\n\n**Services:**\n- Enroll or change contributions\n- Update investment elections\n- Check account balance\n- Model retirement scenarios\n- Request distributions or loans\n- Update beneficiaries\n- Access educational resources\n\n### 14.2 Amazon HR/Benefits\n\n**Benefits Team:**\n- Phone: (555) 123-4570\n- Email: benefits@weaverenterprises.com\n\n**For Questions About:**\n- Eligibility\n- Company match\n- Pay and contribution calculations\n- Leave of absence impact on Plan\n- QDRO reviews\n\n### 14.3 Additional Resources\n\n**Annual Statements:**\n- Mailed each February for prior year\n- Also available online anytime\n\n**Quarterly Statements:**\n- Available online\n- Opt in for paper delivery (fee applies)\n\n**Mobile App:**\n- Download Fidelity NetBenefits app\n- Check balance and make changes on the go\n\n---\n\n**This document is a summary only. The official Plan Document governs in case of any conflict. Amazon reserves the right to amend or terminate the Plan at any time.**\n\n**Plan Name:** Amazon Inc. 401(k) Retirement Savings Plan  \n**Plan Number:** 001  \n**Plan Year:** Calendar Year (January 1 - December 31)  \n**Trustee:** Fidelity Management Trust Company  \n**Plan Administrator:** Amazon Inc., Human Resources Department"}, {"name": "README", "title": "Directory Structure", "content": "# Amazon Recruiting Seed Data\n\nThis directory contains seed data for the Amazon Recruiting scenario.\n\n## Directory Structure\n\n- `org_chart.md` - Organizational chart for all departments (Talent Acquisition, Engineering, Finance, Operations)\n- `recruitment_hiring_policy.md` - Recruiting and hiring policies and procedures\n- `interview_process.md` - Interview guidelines and legal do/don'ts\n- `interview_process_per_job.md` - Detailed interview stages for each open job requisition\n- `onboarding_policy.md` - New-hire onboarding policy and timeline\n- `offer_template.md` - Offer letter template\n- `candidate_faq.md` - Candidate-facing FAQ (high-level)\n- `401K_policy.md` - 401(k) policy details\n- `health_insurance_plan.md` - Health plan summary of benefits and coverage\n- `compensation_bands.json` - Compensation bands for the scenario\n- `skills.md` - HR best practices and behaviors the agent should follow\n- `../candidate_seed/` - Candidate and job requisition seed data\n  - `candidates.json` - Candidate pipeline data (30 candidates)\n  - `resumes/` - Candidate resume files\n  - `frappe_seed/` - Frappe HRMS seed data\n    - `employee_records/` - Employee records (25 employees)\n    - `job_requisitions/` - Job requisition documents (10 open positions)\n\n## Calendar Seeding\n\nCalendar events (interview schedules) can be seeded in two ways:\n\n### 1.
