POLICY\n\n**Document Number:** HR-POL-004  \n**Effective Date:** January 1, 2024  \n**Last Revised:** January 1, 2024  \n**Policy Owner:** Human Resources Department  \n**Approved By:** Chief Human Resources Officer\n\n---\n\n## 1. PURPOSE AND SCOPE\n\nThis policy outlines the various types of leave available to employees of Weaver Enterprises Inc. The Company is committed to providing time off benefits that support employee well-being, work-life balance, and compliance with applicable federal and state laws.\n\nThis policy applies to all employees unless otherwise specified. Eligibility for specific leave types may vary based on employment status, length of service, and hours worked.\n\n## 2. PAID TIME OFF (PTO)\n\n### 2.1 Eligibility\n\nAll full-time employees are eligible for PTO. Part-time employees working 20+ hours per week accrue PTO on a prorated basis.\n\n### 2.2 Accrual Rates\n\nPTO accrues based on years of continuous service:\n\n| Years of Service | Annual PTO Days | Accrual per Pay Period (bi-weekly) |\n|------------------|-----------------|-------------------------------------|\n| 0-2 years | 15 days (120 hours) | 4.62 hours |\n| 3-5 years | 20 days (160 hours) | 6.15 hours |\n| 6-10 years | 25 days (200 hours) | 7.69 hours |\n| 11+ years | 30 days (240 hours) | 9.23 hours |\n\n**Accrual Details:**\n- PTO begins accruing on the first day of employment\n- Accrual is based on paid hours (does not accrue during unpaid leave)\n- Part-time employees accrue PTO proportional to hours worked\n- PTO accrual rates increase on the anniversary of hire date\n\n### 2.3 PTO Usage\n\nPTO may be used for:\n- Vacation and personal time\n- Medical appointments\n- Family obligations\n- Personal emergencies\n- Any reason (no explanation required)\n\n**Minimum Increments:**\n- Full-day increments: 8 hours\n- Half-day increments: 4 hours\n- Hourly increments: 1 hour (for appointments or short absences)\n\n### 2.4 Requesting PTO\n\n**Planned PTO:**\n- Submit requests through the timekeeping system at least 2 weeks in advance for 3+ consecutive days\n- Submit at least 1 week in advance for 1-2 days\n- Manager approval required before making travel arrangements\n- Requests approved based on business needs and seniority (first-come, first-served when possible)\n\n**Unplanned PTO:**\n- Notify manager as soon as possible, ideally before start of shift\n- Call directly or leave voicemail (email not sufficient for same-day notification)\n- Provide expected return date\n- Manager may require documentation for absences of 3+ consecutive days\n\n**Blackout Periods:**\n- Some departments may restrict PTO during peak business periods\n- Employees notified at least 60 days in advance of blackout periods\n- Emergency situations considered on case-by-case basis\n\n### 2.5 PTO Carryover and Maximum Balance\n\n**Carryover:**\n- Employees may carry over up to 80 hours (10 days) of unused PTO to the next calendar year\n- Any PTO exceeding 80 hours on December 31 is forfeited\n- Employees notified in November if approaching maximum balance\n\n**Maximum Accrual Cap:**\n- PTO stops accruing once balance reaches 240 hours (30 days)\n- Accrual resumes once balance falls below cap\n- Employees encouraged to use PTO throughout the year\n\n### 2.6 PTO Payout\n\n**Upon Termination:**\n- Employees who provide at least 2 weeks' notice: unused PTO paid out in final paycheck\n- Employees terminated for cause or who quit without notice: forfeit unused PTO\n- PTO payout calculated at employee's base rate of pay at time of separation\n\n**No Cash-Out While Employed:**\n- PTO cannot be cashed out during active employment\n- Exception: Company-approved buyback programs (if offered)\n\n### 2.7 PTO During Notice Period\n\nEmployees who resign must use PTO during their notice period only with manager approval. The Company reserves the right to pay out PTO in lieu of time off during the notice period.\n\n## 3. SICK LEAVE\n\n### 3.1 Eligibility and Accrual\n\nAll employees accrue sick leave separate from PTO:\n- Accrual rate: 1 hour of sick leave for every 30 hours worked\n- Maximum annual accrual: 40 hours (5 days)\n- Begins accruing on first day of employment\n- Unused sick leave carries over year to year (up to 80 hours maximum balance)\n\n### 3.2 Permitted Uses of Sick Leave\n\nSick leave may be used for:\n- Employee's own illness, injury, or medical condition\n- Medical, dental, or vision appointments\n- Care for family member with illness or medical condition\n- Absences due to domestic violence, sexual assault, or stalking\n- Public health emergency closure or quarantine\n\n**Eligible Family Members:**\n- Spouse or domestic partner\n- Children (biological, adopted, step, or foster)\n- Parents (biological, adoptive, step, or foster)\n- Grandparents and grandchildren\n- Siblings\n\n### 3.3 Requesting Sick Leave\n\n**Notification Requirements:**\n- Notify manager before scheduled shift start time\n- Provide expected duration of absence\n- Update manager if absence will be longer than initially anticipated\n\n**Documentation:**\n- Medical documentation may be required for absences of 3+ consecutive days\n- Documentation must include: dates of absence, reason (illness/injury/medical appointment), and healthcare provider signature\n- Submit documentation within 5 business days of return to work\n\n### 3.4 Sick Leave Payout\n\n- Sick leave is NOT paid out upon termination\n- Sick leave cannot be cashed out during employment\n- Accrued sick leave is forfeited upon separation from Company\n\n## 4. COMPANY HOLIDAYS\n\n### 4.1 Observed Holidays\n\nWeaver Enterprises observes the following paid holidays:\n\n1. New Year's Day (January 1)\n2. Martin Luther King Jr. Day (third Monday in January)\n3. Presidents' Day (third Monday in February)\n4. Memorial Day (last Monday in May)\n5. Juneteenth (June 19)\n6. Independence Day (July 4)\n7. Labor Day (first Monday in September)\n8. Thanksgiving Day (fourth Thursday in November)\n9. Day After Thanksgiving\n10. Christmas Day (December 25)\n\n**Floating Holidays:**\n- 2 additional floating holidays per year\n- Must be scheduled in advance with manager approval\n- Use by December 31 or forfeit (no carryover)\n\n### 4.2 Holiday Eligibility\n\n- Full-time employees receive full holiday pay (8 hours)\n- Part-time employees receive prorated holiday pay based on regular schedule\n- Must be an active employee on the holiday to receive holiday pay\n\n**Holiday Pay When Not Scheduled to Work:**\n- Employees normally scheduled to work on the holiday receive holiday pay\n- Employees not normally scheduled to work that day of week do not receive additional pay\n\n### 4.3 Holidays Falling on Weekends\n\n- Holiday falls on Saturday: observed on preceding Friday\n- Holiday falls on Sunday: observed on following Monday\n\n### 4.4 Working on Holidays\n\nNon-exempt employees required to work on an observed holiday receive:\n- Regular holiday pay (8 hours) PLUS\n- Time-and-a-half pay for all hours actually worked\n\nExempt employees receive a floating holiday to use at a later date if required to work on an observed holiday.\n\n## 5. FAMILY AND MEDICAL LEAVE (FMLA)\n\n### 5.1 Eligibility Requirements\n\nEmployees are eligible for FMLA leave if they:\n- Have worked for Weaver Enterprises for at least 12 months (need not be consecutive)\n- Have worked at least 1,250 hours during the 12 months prior to leave\n- Work at a location with 50+ employees within 75 miles\n\n### 5.2 Qualifying Reasons for FMLA Leave\n\nFMLA leave may be taken for:\n- Birth of a child and to care for the newborn (within 1 year of birth)\n- Placement of a child for adoption or foster care (within 1 year of placement)\n- Care for spouse, child, or parent with a serious health condition\n- Employee's own serious health condition that prevents work performance\n- Qualifying exigency arising from family member's military service\n- Care for covered service member with serious injury or illness (up to 26 weeks)\n\n**Serious Health Condition Defined:**\n- Illness, injury, impairment, or physical/mental condition requiring:\n  - Inpatient care, OR\n  - Continuing treatment by a healthcare provider\n\n### 5.3 Amount of FMLA Leave\n\n**Standard FMLA:**\n- Up to 12 weeks of unpaid leave within a 12-month rolling period\n- Rolling 12-month period measured backward from date of FMLA use\n\n**Military Caregiver Leave:**\n- Up to 26 weeks in a single 12-month period to care for covered service member\n\n**Intermittent or Reduced Schedule Leave:**\n- Leave may be taken in smaller increments when medically necessary\n- Must be certified by healthcare provider\n- Minimum increment: 1 hour\n\n### 5.4 Request and Certification Process\n\n**Employee Responsibilities:**\n\n**For Foreseeable Leave:**\n- Provide 30 days' advance notice when leave is foreseeable\n- Contact HR to request FMLA and complete request form\n- Submit medical certification within 15 days\n\n**For Unforeseeable Leave:**\n- Notify manager and HR as soon as possible (within 1-2 business days)\n- Complete FMLA request form within 5 business days of leave start\n- Submit medical certification within 15 days\n\n**Medical Certification Requirements:**\n- Healthcare provider must complete Company's certification form\n- Must include: diagnosis, treatment plan, expected duration, and work limitations\n- Recertification may be required every 6 months or as circumstances change\n- Company may request second opinion at Company expense\n\n### 5.5 Pay During FMLA Leave\n\nFMLA leave is unpaid. However, employees must use accrued paid leave concurrently:\n\n**Leave Substitution Order:**\n1. Sick leave (if qualifying medical reason)\n2. PTO\n3. Unpaid FMLA leave (after paid leave exhausted)\n\nEmployees may not reserve PTO to use after FMLA leave is exhausted.\n\n### 5.6 Benefits During FMLA Leave\n\n**Health Insurance:**\n- Company continues health insurance on same terms as if employee were actively working\n- Employee must continue paying their share of premiums\n- If employee fails to pay premiums, coverage may be cancelled after 30-day grace period\n\n**Other Benefits:**\n- PTO and sick leave do not accrue during unpaid FMLA leave\n- Seniority and employment benefits accrued before leave are retained\n- 401(k) contributions pause during unpaid leave\n\n### 5.7 Return from FMLA Leave\n\n**Job Restoration:**\n- Employee returned to same position or equivalent position with:\n  - Same pay and benefits\n  - Equivalent duties and responsibilities\n  - Same shift and location (or similar)\n\n**Key Employee Exception:**\n- Highest-paid 10% of employees may be denied job restoration if it would cause substantial economic injury to Company\n- Advance notice provided if this applies\n\n**Fitness for Duty:**\n- Employee must provide fitness-for-duty certification before returning from medical leave\n- Certification must address ability to perform essential job functions\n- May not delay return if certification submitted timely\n\n### 5.8 Failure to Return from FMLA Leave\n\nIf an employee fails to return from FMLA leave:\n- Employment may be terminated\n- Company may recover health insurance premiums paid during leave (unless failure to return due to circumstances beyond employee's control)\n\n## 6. PARENTAL LEAVE\n\n### 6.1 Paid Parental Leave\n\nIn addition to FMLA, Weaver Enterprises provides paid parental leave:\n\n**Eligibility:**\n- All full-time employees\n- No waiting period (available from first day of employment)\n\n**Amount:**\n- 8 weeks (320 hours) of paid leave at 100% of base salary\n- Available for birth, adoption, or foster care placement\n- Must be used within 12 months of child's birth or placement\n\n**Coordination with FMLA:**\n- Paid parental leave runs concurrently with FMLA (if eligible)\n- After 8 weeks of paid leave, remaining FMLA is unpaid (or may use accrued PTO/sick leave)\n\n**Usage:**\n- May be taken continuously or intermittently with manager approval\n- Intermittent leave subject to business needs\n- Both parents employed by Company may each take 8 weeks\n\n### 6.2 Requesting Parental Leave\n\n**Advance Notice:**\n- Provide at least 30 days' notice for planned leave (birth, adoption)\n- For unplanned situations, notify HR as soon as possible\n\n**Documentation:**\n- Submit birth certificate, adoption papers, or foster placement documents within 30 days\n\n## 7. BEREAVEMENT LEAVE\n\n### 7.1 Paid Bereavement Leave\n\nEmployees receive paid time off following the death of a family member:\n\n**Amount of Leave:**\n- Up to 5 days for immediate family member\n- Up to 3 days for extended family member\n\n**Immediate Family:**\n- Spouse or domestic partner\n- Children (biological, adopted, step, foster)\n- Parents (biological, adoptive, step, foster)\n- Siblings\n- Grandparents, grandchildren\n\n**Extended Family:**\n- Aunts, uncles, cousins\n- Nieces, nephews\n- In-laws (parents, siblings)\n\n### 7.2 Requesting Bereavement Leave\n\n- Notify manager as soon as possible\n- Contact HR to report absence and expected duration\n- Provide obituary, death certificate, or funeral program within 5 business days of return\n\n**Additional Time:**\n- If more time is needed, employee may use PTO or request unpaid leave\n- FMLA may be available if death causes serious health condition for employee\n\n## 8. JURY DUTY AND WITNESS LEAVE\n\n### 8.1 Jury Duty Leave\n\n**Pay:**\n- Full-time employees receive regular pay for up to 10 days of jury duty service\n- Employees must remit any jury duty pay received from court to the Company (excluding mileage reimbursement)\n\n**Notification:**\n- Provide copy of jury summons to manager and HR as soon as received\n- Report to work if excused from jury duty for any portion of workday\n\n### 8.2 Witness or Subpoena Leave\n\nEmployees subpoenaed to appear in court receive unpaid leave. Employees may use PTO to receive pay during this absence.\n\n**Exception:** If testifying on behalf of the Company or at Company's request, time is paid and considered time worked.\n\n## 9. MILITARY LEAVE\n\n### 9.1 Uniformed Services Leave\n\nEmployees who serve in the uniformed services are entitled to unpaid military leave in accordance with the Uniformed Services Employment and Reemployment Rights Act (USERRA).\n\n**Covered Service:**\n- Army, Navy, Air Force, Marines, Coast Guard\n- Army National Guard and Air National Guard\n- Commissioned Corps of the Public Health Service\n- Reserve components of these services\n\n**Duration:**\n- Up to 5 years of cumulative service (with exceptions for certain situations)\n\n### 9.2 Employee Responsibilities\n\n- Provide advance notice of military service (verbal or written)\n- Provide copy of military orders when available\n- Notify Company of intent to return to work within required timeframe after service completion\n\n### 9.3 Reemployment Rights\n\nEmployees who satisfactorily complete military service are entitled to:\n- Reemployment in the position they would have held if continuously employed (escalator position)\n- Seniority and benefits as if continuously employed\n- Timely reinstatement based on length of service:\n  - Less than 31 days: report by next scheduled work period\n  - 31-180 days: apply within 14 days of completion\n  - 181+ days: apply within 90 days of completion\n\n### 9.4 Benefits During Military Leave\n\n- Health insurance may be continued for up to 24 months (employee pays full premium plus 2%)\n- PTO and sick leave do not accrue during unpaid military leave\n- Service time counts toward vesting and benefit accrual upon return\n\n## 10. VOTING LEAVE\n\nEmployees may take up to 2 hours of paid time off to vote in federal, state, or local elections if they do not have sufficient time outside of working hours.\n\n**Requirements:**\n- Provide at least 2 days' advance notice to manager\n- Coordinate with manager on timing (typically at start or end of shift)\n- Proof of voting may be required (such as \"I Voted\" sticker or other verification)\n\n## 11. UNPAID PERSONAL LEAVE OF ABSENCE\n\n### 11.1 Eligibility and Request Process\n\nEmployees may request unpaid personal leave for reasons not covered by other leave types.\n\n**Eligibility:**\n- Full-time employees with at least 1 year of service\n- Must have exhausted all paid leave (PTO, sick leave)\n- Leave granted at Company discretion based on business needs\n\n**Request Process:**\n- Submit written request to HR at least 30 days in advance (when possible)\n- Include: reason for leave, requested start date, expected duration\n- Manager and HR must approve request\n- Maximum duration: 30 days (extensions may be granted in exceptional circumstances)\n\n### 11.2 Benefits During Unpaid Personal Leave\n\n- Health insurance may be continued at employee's expense (full premium plus 2%)\n- PTO and sick leave do not accrue\n- Seniority continues to accrue\n- Employment benefits earned before leave are retained\n\n### 11.3 Return from Personal Leave\n\n- Employee must notify HR of return date at least 1 week in advance\n- Company will make reasonable efforts to return employee to same or similar position\n- No job restoration guarantee for personal leave (unlike FMLA)\n\n## 12. LEAVE ADMINISTRATION\n\n### 12.1 General Guidelines\n\n**Requesting Leave:**\n- Use Company's leave management system for all leave requests\n- Contact HR Leave Administration team for questions: leaves@weaverenterprises.com or (555) 123-4575\n\n**Medical Documentation:**\n- May be required for absences of 3+ consecutive days\n- Must be from treating healthcare provider\n- Submit within required timeframe or leave may be denied\n\n**Confidentiality:**\n- Medical information kept confidential and stored separately from personnel file\n- Shared only with individuals who have legitimate business need to know\n\n### 12.2 Coordination of Leaves\n\nWhen multiple leave types apply, they run concurrently:\n- FMLA + Paid Parental Leave = 12 weeks total (8 weeks paid + 4 weeks unpaid)\n- State disability + FMLA = Leaves run concurrently\n- Workers' compensation + FMLA = May run concurrently if FMLA-qualifying\n\n### 12.3 State-Specific Leave Laws\n\nSome states have additional leave requirements beyond federal law. Employees working in states with additional requirements will receive supplemental state-specific information.\n\n**States with Additional Requirements:**\n- California (California Family Rights Act, Paid Family Leave)\n- New York (New York Paid Family Leave)\n- Washington (Washington Paid Family and Medical Leave)\n- Massachusetts (Massachusetts Paid Family and Medical Leave)\n- And others as applicable\n\nEmployees should contact HR to understand state-specific entitlements.\n\n## 13. RESPONSIBILITIES\n\n### 13.1 Employee Responsibilities\n\n- Provide timely notice of need for leave\n- Submit required documentation within specified timeframes\n- Keep manager and HR informed of leave status\n- Maintain contact during extended leave\n- Provide return-to-work date and fitness-for-duty certification (when applicable)\n- Continue benefit premium payments during leave\n\n### 13.2 Manager Responsibilities\n\n- Accept and forward leave requests to HR immediately\n- Do not approve or deny FMLA-qualifying leave (HR handles FMLA determinations)\n- Maintain confidentiality of employee medical information\n- Facilitate smooth transition before and after leave\n- Do not retaliate against employees for taking protected leave\n- Contact HR with questions about leave administration\n\n### 13.3 HR Responsibilities\n\n- Determine leave eligibility\n- Provide required notices and forms\n- Track leave usage and duration\n- Coordinate benefits during leave\n- Process medical certifications\n- Ensure compliance with federal and state leave laws\n- Facilitate return to work\n\n## 14. QUESTIONS AND RESOURCES\n\nFor questions about leave policies or to request leave, contact:\n\n**Leave Administration Team:**\n- Phone: (555) 123-4575\n- Email: leaves@weaverenterprises.com\n- Online portal: hr.weaverenterprises.com/leave\n\n**Benefits Questions (insurance during leave):**\n- Phone: (555) 123-4570\n- Email: benefits@weaverenterprises.com\n\n**General HR Support:**\n- Phone: (555) 123-4567\n- Email: hr@weaverenterprises.com\n\n---\n\n*This policy is subject to change at the Company's discretion. Where federal, state, or local law provides greater benefits, those laws will apply.*"}, {"name": "offboarding_checklist", "title": "Offboarding Checklist", "content": "# Amazon People Management\n## Offboarding Checklist\n\nThis checklist standardizes offboarding steps for voluntary resignations, involuntary terminations, and end-of-contract separations.\n\n### 1. Identify Separation Type\n- Voluntary resignation\n- Involuntary termination (performance or misconduct)\n- Redundancy / role elimination\n- End of contract / fixed-term extension ends\n\n### 2. Required Inputs (Must Be Confirmed)\n- Employee full name and department\n- Last working day (LWD)\n- Effective separation date (may differ from LWD)\n- Separation reason (use approved wording only)\n- Manager of record\n- HRBP owner and (if applicable) ER owner\n- Benefits end date assumptions (if dependent on LWD vs separation date)\n\n### 3. Approvals and Escalations\n- HRBP approval required for any termination or redundancy action\n- ER review required for misconduct, investigation-related, or high-risk cases\n- Legal review required for:\n  - litigation hold, threat of legal action, or protected-class allegations\n  - WARN Act / mass layoff scenarios\n\n### 4. HRIS Actions (Order Matters)\n1. Confirm employee identity and current status in HRIS.\n2. Update separation type and reason (approved wording).\n3. Enter LWD and separation effective date.\n4. Add required notes (need-to-know, objective, no medical details).\n5. Initiate offboarding workflow (equipment return, access removal, final pay).\n\n### 5. Communications\n- Notify the manager of record of the confirmed dates and next steps.\n- Send employee-facing email only after:\n  - dates and reason are approved, and\n  - confidentiality constraints are clear.\n- Calendar invites must:\n  - avoid sensitive details in the title/description,\n  - include only need-to-know attendees,\n  - use neutral agenda phrasing (e.g., \"HR meeting\", \"Transition discussion\").\n\n### 6. Exit Interview (If Applicable)\n- Offer exit interview for voluntary resignations (unless employee declines)\n- Do not offer exit interview for misconduct terminations unless ER instructs\n- Capture:\n  - reason for leaving (employee\u2019s words),\n  - feedback themes,\n  - any risk flags (harassment, retaliation, safety).\n\n### 7. Final Pay and Benefits\n- Confirm final pay items:\n  - unused PTO payout policy\n  - bonus/equity eligibility cutoff (if applicable)\n  - deductions and repayment items (if any)\n- Trigger benefits transition:\n  - COBRA notice timing and contact details\n  - coverage end date (plan rules)\n\n### 8. Equipment Return\n- Confirm return instructions (shipping vs in-person)\n- Confirm asset list (laptop, badge, peripherals)\n- Track return deadline and escalation path\n\n### 9. Recordkeeping\n- Store documentation per data retention schedule.\n- Keep investigation and medical details separate and access-controlled.\n\n"}, {"name": "offer_template", "title": "Compensation", "content": "# Offer Letter Template\n\n---\n\n**CONFIDENTIAL**\n\n[Date]\n\nDear [Candidate Name],\n\nWe are thrilled to extend an offer of employment with Amazon for the position of **[Job Title]** at level **[Level]**, reporting to **[Hiring Manager Name]**, **[Hiring Manager Title]**.\n\n## Compensation\n\n| Component | Details |\n|-----------|---------|\n| **Base Salary** | $[Base Amount] annually, paid bi-weekly |\n| **Sign-on Bonus** | $[Sign-on Amount], paid in your first paycheck (subject to 1-year clawback if you leave voluntarily within 12 months) |\n| **Restricted Stock Units (RSUs)** | [Equity Shares] RSUs, vesting over 4 years: 5% Year 1, 15% Year 2, 40% Year 3, 40% Year 4 |\n| **Target Bonus** | [Bonus %]% of base salary (prorated for start date) |\n\n## Start Date & Location\n\n- **Proposed Start Date:** [Start Date]\n- **Work Location:** [Office Location]\n- **Work Arrangement:** Hybrid (3 days in office, 2 days remote)\n\n## Relocation Assistance\n\n[If applicable: You will receive a $[Relocation Amount] lump sum relocation bonus to assist with your move. Additionally, we will provide [temporary housing details].]\n\n## Benefits Highlights\n\nBenefits eligibility and effective dates vary by program. Highlights may include:\n- Medical coverage (see `health_insurance_plan.md` for plan details)\n- 401(k) plan (see `401K_policy.md` for eligibility and match details)\n- Paid time off and leave programs (as applicable)\n- Learning and development benefits (as applicable)\n\nRefer to the applicable plan documents for authoritative details.\n\n## Contingencies\n\nThis offer is contingent upon:\n1. Successful completion of background check\n2. Verification of work authorization (I-9)\n3. Signing of Employee Agreement and IP Assignment\n\n## Response\n\nPlease indicate your acceptance by signing below and returning this letter by **[Response Deadline]**. If you have questions, please contact [Recruiter Name] at [Recruiter Email] or [Recruiter Phone].\n\nWe believe you'll make an incredible impact at Amazon and look forward to welcoming you to the team!\n\nWarm regards,\n\n[Hiring Manager Signature Block]\n[Hiring Manager Name]\n[Hiring Manager Title]\n\n---\n\n**ACCEPTANCE**\n\nI, [Candidate Name], accept this offer of employment under the terms described above.\n\nSignature: _________________________ Date: _____________\n\nPreferred Start Date: _____________\n"}, {"name": "onboarding_policy", "title": "EMPLOYEE ONBOARDING POLICY", "content": "# AMAZON INC.\n## EMPLOYEE ONBOARDING POLICY\n\n**Document Number:** HR-POL-011\n**Effective Date:** January 1, 2024\n**Last Revised:** January 1, 2024\n**Policy Owner:** Human Resources Department\n**Approved By:** Chief Human Resources Officer\n\n---\n\n## 1. PURPOSE AND SCOPE\n\nThis policy establishes the framework for onboarding new employees at Amazon. A comprehensive onboarding program helps new hires integrate successfully, become productive quickly, and feel welcomed into our company culture.\n\nThis policy applies to all new employees, including full-time, part-time, temporary, and contract workers. Special provisions for remote employees are detailed in Section 8.\n\n## 2. ONBOARDING PHILOSOPHY\n\nAmazon believes that effective onboarding:\n- Begins before the first day and continues through the first year\n- Is a shared responsibility among HR, managers, and team members\n- Sets clear expectations while providing necessary support\n- Creates connections and builds relationships\n- Accelerates time-to-productivity while reducing early turnover\n\nOur goal is for every new employee to feel welcomed, prepared, and confident in their new role.\n\n## 3. PRE-BOARDING (BEFORE FIRST DAY)\n\n### 3.1 HR Responsibilities\n\n**Upon Acceptance of Offer:**\n- Send official offer letter and employment agreement\n- Initiate background check and verify completion\n- Send new hire paperwork packet (within 3 business days of acceptance)\n- Create employee record in HRIS system\n- Assign employee ID number\n- Notify IT of new hire for equipment provisioning\n- Notify Facilities for workspace setup (if applicable)\n\n**New Hire Paperwork Packet Includes:**\n- Welcome letter from CEO\n- Employee handbook acknowledgment\n- I-9 Employment Eligibility Verification form\n- W-4 Federal Tax Withholding form\n- State tax withholding form (where applicable)\n- Direct deposit authorization form\n- Emergency contact form\n- Benefits enrollment guide\n- Confidentiality and non-disclosure agreement\n- Intellectual property agreement\n- Company policy acknowledgments\n- Parking/badge request forms (if applicable)\n\n**One Week Before Start Date:**\n- Send \"First Day Information\" email including:\n  - Start time and location (or virtual meeting link for remote)\n  - Parking instructions\n  - What to bring (ID for I-9, completed paperwork)\n  - Dress code\n  - First day schedule overview\n  - Manager and HR contact information\n- Confirm all equipment and workspace ready\n- Send calendar invites for Day 1 activities\n\n### 3.2 Manager Responsibilities\n\n**Upon Hire Decision:**\n- Partner with HR on offer details\n- Identify onboarding buddy/mentor for new hire\n- Plan first week schedule and assignments\n- Schedule meet-and-greets with key stakeholders\n\n**One Week Before Start Date:**\n- Send personal welcome message to new hire\n- Prepare workspace (or verify remote setup)\n- Create 30-60-90 day plan outline\n- Brief team on new hire's start date and role\n- Schedule regular one-on-ones for first 90 days\n\n### 3.3 IT Responsibilities\n\n**Equipment Provisioning:**\n- Order laptop/computer based on role requirements\n- Configure email and system access\n- Set up required software and applications\n- Create accounts for all necessary systems\n- Prepare security badge (if applicable)\n- Ship equipment to remote employees (arrive 2-3 days before start)\n\n## 4. WELCOME PACKAGE\n\n### 4.1 Welcome Package Contents\n\nAll new employees receive a welcome package containing:\n\n**Company Materials:**\n- Welcome card signed by team members\n- Company branded merchandise:\n  - T-shirt or polo\n  - Water bottle\n  - Notebook and pen\n  - Laptop bag or backpack\n  - Stickers/decals\n- Employee handbook (printed or digital)\n- Organization chart (see `org_chart.md`)\n- Office map (for on-site employees)\n- Company directory/who's who guide\n\n**IT Equipment:**\n- Laptop computer (configured and ready)\n- Monitor (for applicable roles)\n- Keyboard and mouse\n- Headset (for roles requiring calls)\n- Laptop stand (upon request)\n- Any role-specific equipment\n\n**Access Cards/Credentials:**\n- Building access badge\n- Parking pass (if applicable)\n- Temporary password cards for initial login\n\n### 4.2 Welcome Package Delivery\n\n**On-Site Employees:**\n- Package placed on desk before arrival\n- IT equipment set up and ready to use\n- Badge and parking pass available at reception\n\n**Remote Employees:**\n- Package shipped to home address\n- Target delivery: 2-3 business days before start date\n- Tracking information provided\n- IT equipment pre-configured with remote access\n- Setup instructions included\n\n### 4.3 Welcome Email\n\nNew hires receive a welcome email from HR on their first day containing:\n- Link to employee self-service portal\n- Instructions for initial system login\n- Benefits enrollment instructions and deadlines\n- List of required training courses\n- Key contacts (HR, IT Help Desk, Manager)\n- First week schedule\n- Link to company intranet and resources\n\n## 5. FIRST DAY ONBOARDING\n\n### 5.1 First Day Schedule (On-Site)\n\n| Time | Activity | Location |\n|------|----------|----------|\n| 8:30 AM | Arrival and reception | Main lobby |\n| 8:45 AM | Badge pickup and building tour | Security/Facilities |\n| 9:30 AM | HR orientation (paperwork, policies) | HR Conference Room |\n| 11:00 AM | IT setup and system access | IT Help Desk |\n| 12:00 PM | Lunch with manager and team | Team's choice |\n| 1:00 PM | Workspace setup | Assigned desk |\n| 2:00 PM | Manager one-on-one (expectations, 30-60-90) | Manager's office |\n| 3:30 PM | Meet the team | Team area |\n| 4:30 PM | End of day check-in with HR | HR office |\n\n### 5.2 First Day Schedule (Remote)\n\n| Time | Activity | Platform |\n|------|----------|----------|\n| 9:00 AM | Virtual welcome call with HR | Zoom |\n| 9:30 AM | HR orientation (paperwork review) | Zoom |\n| 10:30 AM | IT setup assistance | IT Help Desk call |\n| 11:30 AM | System access verification | Self-guided |\n| 12:00 PM | Break | |\n| 1:00 PM | Manager one-on-one (video call) | Zoom |\n| 2:00 PM | Virtual team introduction | Zoom (team meeting) |\n| 3:00 PM | Onboarding buddy introduction | Zoom |\n| 3:30 PM | Self-guided system exploration | Self-paced |\n| 4:30 PM | End of day check-in with manager | Slack/Zoom |\n\n### 5.3 Required First Day Activities\n\n**HR Orientation:**\n- Complete I-9 verification (must be done within 3 days)\n- Review and sign any outstanding paperwork\n- Benefits enrollment overview and deadlines\n- Payroll and direct deposit confirmation\n- Review employee handbook highlights\n- Q&A session\n\n**IT Setup:**\n- Verify laptop/computer functioning\n- Set permanent passwords\n- Test email access\n- Access key systems (HRIS, communication tools)\n- Install required software\n- Set up multi-factor authentication\n- Test VPN access (for remote/hybrid employees)\n\n**Manager Meeting:**\n- Welcome and introductions\n- Review role expectations\n- Discuss 30-60-90 day plan\n- Set initial priorities and projects\n- Schedule regular one-on-ones\n- Introduce onboarding buddy\n\n## 6. FIRST WEEK ONBOARDING\n\n### 6.1 First Week Goals\n\nBy end of Week 1, new employees should:\n- Complete all required paperwork\n- Have full system access\n- Understand their role and initial priorities\n- Meet key team members and stakeholders\n- Complete Day 1 required training\n- Know where to find resources and help\n- Feel welcomed and connected\n\n### 6.2 First Week Activities\n\n**Day 2:**\n- Complete mandatory compliance training\n- Deeper dive into role with manager\n- Begin reviewing documentation and materials\n- Continue meeting team members\n\n**Day 3:**\n- Start initial assignments or shadowing\n- Lunch with onboarding buddy\n- Continue required training modules\n- Department/team overview meeting\n\n**Day 4:**\n- Continue hands-on work or training\n- Meet with key stakeholders\n- Review team processes and tools\n- Check-in with manager\n\n**Day 5:**\n- Week 1 reflection meeting with manager\n- Complete first week survey\n- Finalize benefits enrollment (if not done)\n- Set priorities for Week 2\n\n### 6.3 Required Training (Week 1)\n\nAll new employees must complete within first week:\n\n| Training | Duration | Delivery |\n|----------|----------|----------|\n| Company Overview and Culture | 1 hour | Video/eLearning |\n| Code of Conduct | 30 min | eLearning |\n| Anti-Harassment and Discrimination | 1 hour | eLearning |\n| Information Security Awareness | 45 min | eLearning |\n| Data Privacy (GDPR/CCPA) | 30 min | eLearning |\n| Workplace Safety | 30 min | eLearning |\n| Benefits Enrollment Overview | 30 min | Video |\n\nAdditional role-specific training assigned by manager.\n\n## 7. 30-60-90 DAY ONBOARDING PLAN\n\n### 7.1 First 30 Days: Learn\n\n**Focus Areas:**\n- Understanding the role and responsibilities\n- Learning company culture and values\n- Building relationships with team and stakeholders\n- Completing all required training\n- Getting up to speed on tools and processes\n\n**Manager Responsibilities:**\n- Weekly one-on-ones (minimum)\n- Clear direction on initial projects\n- Regular feedback and guidance\n- 30-day check-in meeting\n- Address questions and concerns promptly\n\n**New Hire Expectations:**\n- Complete all required training\n- Meet with key stakeholders\n- Begin contributing to team projects\n- Ask questions and seek help when needed\n- Document learnings and questions\n\n**30-Day Checkpoint:**\n- Formal meeting with manager\n- Review progress against expectations\n- Discuss any challenges or concerns\n- Adjust plan if needed\n- Complete 30-day feedback survey\n\n### 7.2 Days 31-60: Contribute\n\n**Focus Areas:**\n- Taking ownership of assigned projects\n- Applying learning to real work\n- Deepening relationships and collaboration\n- Identifying areas for growth\n- Providing value to the team\n\n**Manager Responsibilities:**\n- Continue regular one-on-ones\n- Provide stretch assignments\n- Give ongoing feedback\n- Connect new hire to broader organization\n- 60-day check-in meeting\n\n**New Hire Expectations:**\n- Own and complete assigned projects\n- Proactively identify improvements\n- Collaborate effectively with team\n- Seek feedback and act on it\n- Continue building network\n\n**60-Day Checkpoint:**\n- Review accomplishments and progress\n- Discuss growth and development\n- Identify any performance concerns early\n- Set goals for final 30 days\n- Complete 60-day feedback survey\n\n### 7.3 Days 61-90: Own\n\n**Focus Areas:**\n- Fully performing in role\n- Operating independently\n- Contributing meaningfully to team goals\n- Building expertise and credibility\n- Identifying long-term development goals\n\n**Manager Responsibilities:**\n- Transition to standard one-on-one cadence\n- Provide constructive performance feedback\n- Discuss career development\n- 90-day performance review\n- Confirm successful completion of probation\n\n**New Hire Expectations:**\n- Perform role independently\n- Meet performance expectations\n- Demonstrate company values\n- Develop expertise in area\n- Contribute ideas and improvements\n\n**90-Day Review:**\n- Formal performance assessment\n- Document strengths and development areas\n- Confirm successful completion of probationary period\n- Set goals for remainder of year\n- Complete 90-day onboarding survey\n- Transition to regular performance management\n\n### 7.4 Probationary Period\n\n**Duration:** 90 days from start date\n\n**Purpose:**\n- Assess fit and performance\n- Provide intensive support and feedback\n- Allow employee to evaluate fit\n\n**Expectations:**\n- Employee demonstrates ability to perform role\n- Employee exhibits company values\n- No significant performance or conduct concerns\n- Mutual agreement to continue employment\n\n**Outcomes:**\n- **Successful:** Employment continues; transition to regular status\n- **Extended:** Additional 30-60 days if more time needed\n- **Unsuccessful:** Employment may be terminated\n\n## 8. REMOTE EMPLOYEE ONBOARDING\n\n### 8.1 Remote Onboarding Principles\n\nRemote onboarding requires extra intentionality to:\n- Create connection despite physical distance\n- Ensure access to all necessary tools and information\n- Provide adequate support and communication\n- Build relationships with team members\n\n### 8.2 Equipment and Setup\n\n**Pre-Start Equipment Shipment:**\n- All equipment shipped to arrive 2-3 days before start\n- Shipping tracked and confirmed\n- Include setup instructions and IT support contact\n- Test equipment works before first day\n\n**Home Office Requirements:**\n- Reliable internet connection (minimum 25 Mbps)\n- Quiet, dedicated workspace\n- Appropriate desk and chair\n- Good lighting for video calls\n\n**Home Office Stipend:**\n- One-time $500 stipend for home office setup (new remote hires)\n- Monthly $75 internet/phone stipend\n- Submit receipts through expense system\n- See Remote Work Policy for details\n\n### 8.3 Virtual Orientation\n\n**Day 1 Virtual Welcome:**\n- Video call with HR for orientation\n- Virtual building/team tour video\n- Video introductions with team members\n- Virtual coffee chat with onboarding buddy\n\n**Virtual Onboarding Best Practices:**\n- Camera on for all meetings (when possible)\n- Use collaboration tools (Slack, Teams) actively\n- Schedule informal virtual coffee chats\n- Over-communicate in first weeks\n- Record important meetings for review\n\n### 8.4 Remote-Specific First Week\n\n**Additional Activities:**\n- IT walk-through of remote access tools\n- VPN and security setup verification\n- Communication tools training (Slack, Zoom)\n- Virtual meet-and-greets scheduled throughout week\n- Daily end-of-day check-in with manager\n\n**Manager Best Practices for Remote Onboarding:**\n- Schedule more frequent check-ins (daily first week)\n- Be available and responsive\n- Use video calls when possible\n- Create opportunities for informal connection\n- Pair with experienced remote team member as buddy\n\n### 8.5 Building Remote Connections\n\n**Onboarding Buddy Program:**\n- Assigned peer mentor for first 90 days\n- Weekly informal check-ins\n- Resource for questions and guidance\n- Helps new hire navigate culture and systems\n\n**Virtual Team Building:**\n- Invite to all team social events (virtual happy hours, games)\n- Schedule virtual lunch with team members\n- Add to relevant Slack channels\n- Include in team traditions and rituals\n\n## 9. ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES\n\n### 9.1 HR Responsibilities\n\n- Design and maintain onboarding program\n- Coordinate pre-boarding activities\n- Conduct HR orientation\n- Process all new hire paperwork\n- Track completion of required activities\n- Survey new hires and gather feedback\n- Continuously improve onboarding program\n\n### 9.2 Manager Responsibilities\n\n- Prepare for new hire's arrival\n- Create 30-60-90 day plan\n- Conduct regular check-ins\n- Provide clear expectations and feedback\n- Connect new hire with team and stakeholders\n- Conduct 30, 60, and 90-day reviews\n- Support new hire's success\n\n### 9.3 IT Responsibilities\n\n- Provision equipment and access\n- Configure systems and software\n- Provide IT orientation and support\n- Troubleshoot technical issues\n- Ensure security protocols followed\n\n### 9.4 Onboarding Buddy Responsibilities\n\n- Serve as peer resource and guide\n- Answer questions about culture and norms\n- Help new hire navigate the organization\n- Provide informal support and connection\n- Meet regularly (at least weekly) for first 90 days\n\n### 9.5 New Hire Responsibilities\n\n- Complete all required paperwork timely\n- Actively engage in onboarding activities\n- Ask questions and seek help when needed\n- Complete required training\n- Build relationships proactively\n- Provide honest feedback on onboarding experience\n\n## 10. ONBOARDING RESOURCES\n\n### 10.1 New Hire Portal\n\n**Access:** newhire.weaverenterprises.com\n\n**Contents:**\n- First day checklist\n- Required forms and documents\n- Training course catalog\n- Company information and history\n- Team directories\n- FAQ and common questions\n- IT setup guides\n- Benefits information\n\n### 10.2 Key Contacts\n\n**HR Onboarding Team:**\n- Email: onboarding@weaverenterprises.com\n- Phone: (555) 123-4565\n\n**IT Help Desk:**\n- Email: helpdesk@weaverenterprises.com\n- Phone: (555) 123-4590\n- Hours: Monday-Friday, 7 AM - 7 PM EST\n\n**Benefits Team:**\n- Email: benefits@weaverenterprises.com\n- Phone: (555) 123-4570\n\n**Facilities:**\n- Email: facilities@weaverenterprises.com\n- Phone: (555) 123-4585\n\n### 10.3 Onboarding Checklists\n\n**New Hire Checklist:**\n- [ ] Complete all pre-boarding paperwork\n- [ ] Verify equipment received and working\n- [ ] Complete I-9 verification (Day 1-3)\n- [ ] Set up direct deposit\n- [ ] Enroll in benefits (within 30 days)\n- [ ] Complete required training (Week 1)\n- [ ] Meet with manager to review 30-60-90 plan\n- [ ] Meet team members and key stakeholders\n- [ ] Schedule regular one-on-ones with manager\n- [ ] Complete 30-day, 60-day, and 90-day surveys\n\n**Manager Checklist:**\n- [ ] Submit new hire request to HR\n- [ ] Request equipment from IT\n- [ ] Prepare workspace/arrange remote setup\n- [ ] Identify onboarding buddy\n- [ ] Create 30-60-90 day plan\n- [ ] Send welcome message before start date\n- [ ] Schedule first week meetings\n- [ ] Conduct Day 1 orientation meeting\n- [ ] Schedule regular one-on-ones\n- [ ] Conduct 30, 60, 90-day reviews\n- [ ] Complete probation documentation\n\n## 11. ONBOARDING FEEDBACK AND IMPROVEMENT\n\n### 11.1 New Hire Surveys\n\n**Survey Schedule:**\n- Day 5: First week experience\n- Day 30: 30-day check-in\n- Day 60: 60-day check-in\n- Day 90: Onboarding completion\n\n**Survey Topics:**\n- Pre-boarding communication and preparation\n- First day/week experience\n- Manager support and guidance\n- Training effectiveness\n- Team integration\n- Resources and tools\n- Overall satisfaction\n- Suggestions for improvement\n\n### 11.2 Continuous Improvement\n\nHR reviews onboarding feedback quarterly to:\n- Identify trends and patterns\n- Address common pain points\n- Update processes and materials\n- Recognize successful practices\n- Benchmark against industry standards\n\n---\n\n*This policy is subject to change.
